47#define TEXT_DUMP_HEADER "--\n-- PostgreSQL database dump\n--\n\n"
48#define TEXT_DUMPALL_HEADER "--\n-- PostgreSQL database cluster dump\n--\n\n"
49
50#define TOC_PREFIX_NONE		""
51#define TOC_PREFIX_DATA		"Data for "
52#define TOC_PREFIX_STATS	"Statistics for "
53
54static ArchiveHandle *_allocAH(const char *FileSpec, const ArchiveFormat fmt,
55							   const pg_compress_specification compression_spec,
56							   bool dosync, ArchiveMode mode,
57							   SetupWorkerPtrType setupWorkerPtr,
58							   DataDirSyncMethod sync_method);
59static void _getObjectDescription(PQExpBuffer buf, const TocEntry *te);
60static void _printTocEntry(ArchiveHandle *AH, TocEntry *te, const char *pfx);
61static void _doSetFixedOutputState(ArchiveHandle *AH);
62static void _doSetSessionAuth(ArchiveHandle *AH, const char *user);
63static void _reconnectToDB(ArchiveHandle *AH, const char *dbname);
64static void _becomeUser(ArchiveHandle *AH, const char *user);
65static void _becomeOwner(ArchiveHandle *AH, TocEntry *te);
66static void _selectOutputSchema(ArchiveHandle *AH, const char *schemaName);
67static void _selectTablespace(ArchiveHandle *AH, const char *tablespace);
68static void _selectTableAccessMethod(ArchiveHandle *AH, const char *tableam);
69static void _printTableAccessMethodNoStorage(ArchiveHandle *AH,
70											 TocEntry *te);
71static void processEncodingEntry(ArchiveHandle *AH, TocEntry *te);
72static void processStdStringsEntry(ArchiveHandle *AH, TocEntry *te);
73static void processSearchPathEntry(ArchiveHandle *AH, TocEntry *te);
74static int	_tocEntryRequired(TocEntry *te, teSection curSection, ArchiveHandle *AH);
75static RestorePass _tocEntryRestorePass(TocEntry *te);
76static bool _tocEntryIsACL(TocEntry *te);
77static void _disableTriggersIfNecessary(ArchiveHandle *AH, TocEntry *te);
78static void _enableTriggersIfNecessary(ArchiveHandle *AH, TocEntry *te);
79static bool is_load_via_partition_root(TocEntry *te);
80static void buildTocEntryArrays(ArchiveHandle *AH);
81static void _moveBefore(TocEntry *pos, TocEntry *te);
82static int	_discoverArchiveFormat(ArchiveHandle *AH);
83
84static int	RestoringToDB(ArchiveHandle *AH);
85static void dump_lo_buf(ArchiveHandle *AH);
86static void dumpTimestamp(ArchiveHandle *AH, const char *msg, time_t tim);
87static void SetOutput(ArchiveHandle *AH, const char *filename,
88					  const pg_compress_specification compression_spec);
89static CompressFileHandle *SaveOutput(ArchiveHandle *AH);
90static void RestoreOutput(ArchiveHandle *AH, CompressFileHandle *savedOutput);
91
92static int	restore_toc_entry(ArchiveHandle *AH, TocEntry *te, bool is_parallel);
93static void restore_toc_entries_prefork(ArchiveHandle *AH,
94										TocEntry *pending_list);
95static void restore_toc_entries_parallel(ArchiveHandle *AH,
96										 ParallelState *pstate,
97										 TocEntry *pending_list);
98static void restore_toc_entries_postfork(ArchiveHandle *AH,
99										 TocEntry *pending_list);
100static void pending_list_header_init(TocEntry *l);
101static void pending_list_append(TocEntry *l, TocEntry *te);
102static void pending_list_remove(TocEntry *te);
103static int	TocEntrySizeCompareQsort(const void *p1, const void *p2);
104static int	TocEntrySizeCompareBinaryheap(void *p1, void *p2, void *arg);
105static void move_to_ready_heap(TocEntry *pending_list,
106							   binaryheap *ready_heap,
107							   RestorePass pass);
108static TocEntry *pop_next_work_item(binaryheap *ready_heap,
109									ParallelState *pstate);
110static void mark_dump_job_done(ArchiveHandle *AH,
111							   TocEntry *te,
112							   int status,
113							   void *callback_data);
114static void mark_restore_job_done(ArchiveHandle *AH,
115								  TocEntry *te,
116								  int status,
117								  void *callback_data);
118static void fix_dependencies(ArchiveHandle *AH);
119static bool has_lock_conflicts(TocEntry *te1, TocEntry *te2);
120static void repoint_table_dependencies(ArchiveHandle *AH);
121static void identify_locking_dependencies(ArchiveHandle *AH, TocEntry *te);
122static void reduce_dependencies(ArchiveHandle *AH, TocEntry *te,
123								binaryheap *ready_heap);
124static void mark_create_done(ArchiveHandle *AH, TocEntry *te);
125static void inhibit_data_for_failed_table(ArchiveHandle *AH, TocEntry *te);
126
127static void StrictNamesCheck(RestoreOptions *ropt);
128
129
130/*
131 * Allocate a new DumpOptions block containing all default values.
132 */
133DumpOptions *
134NewDumpOptions(void)
135{
136	DumpOptions *opts = (DumpOptions *) pg_malloc(sizeof(DumpOptions));
137
138	InitDumpOptions(opts);
139	return opts;
140}
141
142/*
143 * Initialize a DumpOptions struct to all default values
144 */
145void
146InitDumpOptions(DumpOptions *opts)
147{
148	memset(opts, 0, sizeof(DumpOptions));
149	/* set any fields that shouldn't default to zeroes */
150	opts->include_everything = true;
151	opts->cparams.promptPassword = TRI_DEFAULT;
152	opts->dumpSections = DUMP_UNSECTIONED;
153	opts->dumpSchema = true;
154	opts->dumpData = true;
155	opts->dumpStatistics = false;
156}
157
158/*
159 * Create a freshly allocated DumpOptions with options equivalent to those
160 * found in the given RestoreOptions.
161 */
162DumpOptions *
163dumpOptionsFromRestoreOptions(RestoreOptions *ropt)
164{
165	DumpOptions *dopt = NewDumpOptions();
166
167	/* this is the inverse of what's at the end of pg_dump.c's main() */
168	dopt->cparams.dbname = ropt->cparams.dbname ? pg_strdup(ropt->cparams.dbname) : NULL;
169	dopt->cparams.pgport = ropt->cparams.pgport ? pg_strdup(ropt->cparams.pgport) : NULL;
170	dopt->cparams.pghost = ropt->cparams.pghost ? pg_strdup(ropt->cparams.pghost) : NULL;
171	dopt->cparams.username = ropt->cparams.username ? pg_strdup(ropt->cparams.username) : NULL;
172	dopt->cparams.promptPassword = ropt->cparams.promptPassword;
173	dopt->outputClean = ropt->dropSchema;
174	dopt->dumpData = ropt->dumpData;
175	dopt->dumpSchema = ropt->dumpSchema;
176	dopt->dumpSections = ropt->dumpSections;
177	dopt->dumpStatistics = ropt->dumpStatistics;
178	dopt->if_exists = ropt->if_exists;
179	dopt->column_inserts = ropt->column_inserts;
180	dopt->aclsSkip = ropt->aclsSkip;
181	dopt->outputSuperuser = ropt->superuser;
182	dopt->outputCreateDB = ropt->createDB;
183	dopt->outputNoOwner = ropt->noOwner;
184	dopt->outputNoTableAm = ropt->noTableAm;
185	dopt->outputNoTablespaces = ropt->noTablespace;
186	dopt->disable_triggers = ropt->disable_triggers;
187	dopt->use_setsessauth = ropt->use_setsessauth;
188	dopt->disable_dollar_quoting = ropt->disable_dollar_quoting;
189	dopt->dump_inserts = ropt->dump_inserts;
190	dopt->no_comments = ropt->no_comments;
191	dopt->no_policies = ropt->no_policies;
192	dopt->no_publications = ropt->no_publications;
193	dopt->no_security_labels = ropt->no_security_labels;
194	dopt->no_subscriptions = ropt->no_subscriptions;
195	dopt->lockWaitTimeout = ropt->lockWaitTimeout;
196	dopt->include_everything = ropt->include_everything;
197	dopt->enable_row_security = ropt->enable_row_security;
198	dopt->sequence_data = ropt->sequence_data;
199	dopt->restrict_key = ropt->restrict_key ? pg_strdup(ropt->restrict_key) : NULL;
200
201	return dopt;
202}
203
204
205/*
206 *	Wrapper functions.
207 *
208 *	The objective is to make writing new formats and dumpers as simple
209 *	as possible, if necessary at the expense of extra function calls etc.
210 *
211 */
212
213/*
214 * The dump worker setup needs lots of knowledge of the internals of pg_dump,
215 * so it's defined in pg_dump.c and passed into OpenArchive. The restore worker
216 * setup doesn't need to know anything much, so it's defined here.
217 */
218static void
219setupRestoreWorker(Archive *AHX)
220{
221	ArchiveHandle *AH = (ArchiveHandle *) AHX;
222
223	AH->ReopenPtr(AH);
224}
225
226
227/* Create a new archive */
228/* Public */
229Archive *
230CreateArchive(const char *FileSpec, const ArchiveFormat fmt,
231			  const pg_compress_specification compression_spec,
232			  bool dosync, ArchiveMode mode,
233			  SetupWorkerPtrType setupDumpWorker,
234			  DataDirSyncMethod sync_method)
235
236{
237	ArchiveHandle *AH = _allocAH(FileSpec, fmt, compression_spec,
238								 dosync, mode, setupDumpWorker, sync_method);
239
240	return (Archive *) AH;
241}
242
243/* Open an existing archive */
244/* Public */
245Archive *
246OpenArchive(const char *FileSpec, const ArchiveFormat fmt)
247{
248	ArchiveHandle *AH;
249	pg_compress_specification compression_spec = {0};
250
251	compression_spec.algorithm = PG_COMPRESSION_NONE;
252	AH = _allocAH(FileSpec, fmt, compression_spec, true,
253				  archModeRead, setupRestoreWorker,
254				  DATA_DIR_SYNC_METHOD_FSYNC);
255
256	return (Archive *) AH;
257}
258
259/* Public */
260void
261CloseArchive(Archive *AHX)
262{
263	ArchiveHandle *AH = (ArchiveHandle *) AHX;
264
265	AH->ClosePtr(AH);
266
267	/* Close the output */
268	errno = 0;
269	if (!EndCompressFileHandle(AH->OF))
270		pg_fatal("could not close output file: %m");
271}
272
273/* Public */
274void
275SetArchiveOptions(Archive *AH, DumpOptions *dopt, RestoreOptions *ropt)
276{
277	/* Caller can omit dump options, in which case we synthesize them */
278	if (dopt == NULL && ropt != NULL)
279		dopt = dumpOptionsFromRestoreOptions(ropt);
280
281	/* Save options for later access */
282	AH->dopt = dopt;
283	AH->ropt = ropt;
284}
285
286/* Public */
287void
288ProcessArchiveRestoreOptions(Archive *AHX)
289{
290	ArchiveHandle *AH = (ArchiveHandle *) AHX;
291	RestoreOptions *ropt = AH->public.ropt;
292	TocEntry   *te;
293	teSection	curSection;
294
295	/* Decide which TOC entries will be dumped/restored, and mark them */
296	curSection = SECTION_PRE_DATA;
297	for (te = AH->toc->next; te != AH->toc; te = te->next)
298	{
299		/*
300		 * When writing an archive, we also take this opportunity to check
301		 * that we have generated the entries in a sane order that respects
302		 * the section divisions.  When reading, don't complain, since buggy
303		 * old versions of pg_dump might generate out-of-order archives.
304		 */
305		if (AH->mode != archModeRead)
306		{
307			switch (te->section)
308			{
309				case SECTION_NONE:
310					/* ok to be anywhere */
311					break;
312				case SECTION_PRE_DATA:
313					if (curSection != SECTION_PRE_DATA)
314						pg_log_warning("archive items not in correct section order");
315					break;
316				case SECTION_DATA:
317					if (curSection == SECTION_POST_DATA)
318						pg_log_warning("archive items not in correct section order");
319					break;
320				case SECTION_POST_DATA:
321					/* ok no matter which section we were in */
322					break;
323				default:
324					pg_fatal("unexpected section code %d",
325							 (int) te->section);
326					break;
327			}
328		}
329
330		if (te->section != SECTION_NONE)
331			curSection = te->section;
332
333		te->reqs = _tocEntryRequired(te, curSection, AH);
334	}
335
336	/* Enforce strict names checking */
337	if (ropt->strict_names)
338		StrictNamesCheck(ropt);
339}
340
341/* Public */
342void
343RestoreArchive(Archive *AHX)
344{
345	ArchiveHandle *AH = (ArchiveHandle *) AHX;
346	RestoreOptions *ropt = AH->public.ropt;
347	bool		parallel_mode;
348	TocEntry   *te;
349	CompressFileHandle *sav;
350
351	AH->stage = STAGE_INITIALIZING;
352
353	/*
354	 * If we're going to do parallel restore, there are some restrictions.
355	 */
356	parallel_mode = (AH->public.numWorkers > 1 && ropt->useDB);
357	if (parallel_mode)
358	{
359		/* We haven't got round to making this work for all archive formats */
360		if (AH->ClonePtr == NULL || AH->ReopenPtr == NULL)
361			pg_fatal("parallel restore is not supported with this archive file format");
362
363		/* Doesn't work if the archive represents dependencies as OIDs */
364		if (AH->version < K_VERS_1_8)
365			pg_fatal("parallel restore is not supported with archives made by pre-8.0 pg_dump");
366
367		/*
368		 * It's also not gonna work if we can't reopen the input file, so
369		 * let's try that immediately.
370		 */
371		AH->ReopenPtr(AH);
372	}
373
374	/*
375	 * Make sure we won't need (de)compression we haven't got
376	 */
377	if (AH->PrintTocDataPtr != NULL)
378	{
379		for (te = AH->toc->next; te != AH->toc; te = te->next)
380		{
381			if (te->hadDumper && (te->reqs & REQ_DATA) != 0)
382			{
383				char	   *errmsg = supports_compression(AH->compression_spec);
384
385				if (errmsg)
386					pg_fatal("cannot restore from compressed archive (%s)",
387							 errmsg);
388				else
389					break;
390			}
391		}
392	}
393
394	/*
395	 * Prepare index arrays, so we can assume we have them throughout restore.
396	 * It's possible we already did this, though.
397	 */
398	if (AH->tocsByDumpId == NULL)
399		buildTocEntryArrays(AH);
400
401	/*
402	 * If we're using a DB connection, then connect it.
403	 */
404	if (ropt->useDB)
405	{
406		pg_log_info("connecting to database for restore");
407		if (AH->version < K_VERS_1_3)
408			pg_fatal("direct database connections are not supported in pre-1.3 archives");
409
410		/*
411		 * We don't want to guess at whether the dump will successfully
412		 * restore; allow the attempt regardless of the version of the restore
413		 * target.
414		 */
415		AHX->minRemoteVersion = 0;
416		AHX->maxRemoteVersion = 9999999;
417
418		ConnectDatabaseAhx(AHX, &ropt->cparams, false);
419
420		/*
421		 * If we're talking to the DB directly, don't send comments since they
422		 * obscure SQL when displaying errors
423		 */
424		AH->noTocComments = 1;
425	}
426
427	/*
428	 * Work out if we have an implied schema-less restore. This can happen if
429	 * the dump excluded the schema or the user has used a toc list to exclude
430	 * all of the schema data. All we do is look for schema entries - if none
431	 * are found then we unset the dumpSchema flag.
432	 *
433	 * We could scan for wanted TABLE entries, but that is not the same as
434	 * data-only. At this stage, it seems unnecessary (6-Mar-2001).
435	 */
436	if (ropt->dumpSchema)
437	{
438		bool		no_schema_found = true;
439
440		for (te = AH->toc->next; te != AH->toc; te = te->next)
441		{
442			if ((te->reqs & REQ_SCHEMA) != 0)
443			{
444				no_schema_found = false;
445				break;
446			}
447		}
448		if (no_schema_found)
449		{
450			ropt->dumpSchema = false;
451			pg_log_info("implied no-schema restore");
452		}
453	}
454
455	/*
456	 * Setup the output file if necessary.
457	 */
458	sav = SaveOutput(AH);
459	if (ropt->filename || ropt->compression_spec.algorithm != PG_COMPRESSION_NONE)
460		SetOutput(AH, ropt->filename, ropt->compression_spec);
461
462	ahprintf(AH, "--\n-- PostgreSQL database dump\n--\n\n");
463
464	/*
465	 * If generating plain-text output, enter restricted mode to block any
466	 * unexpected psql meta-commands.  A malicious source might try to inject
467	 * a variety of things via bogus responses to queries.  While we cannot
468	 * prevent such sources from affecting the destination at restore time, we
469	 * can block psql meta-commands so that the client machine that runs psql
470	 * with the dump output remains unaffected.
471	 */
472	if (ropt->restrict_key)
473		ahprintf(AH, "\\restrict %s\n\n", ropt->restrict_key);
474
475	if (AH->archiveRemoteVersion)
476		ahprintf(AH, "-- Dumped from database version %s\n",
477				 AH->archiveRemoteVersion);
478	if (AH->archiveDumpVersion)
479		ahprintf(AH, "-- Dumped by pg_dump version %s\n",
480				 AH->archiveDumpVersion);
481
482	ahprintf(AH, "\n");
483
484	if (AH->public.verbose)
485		dumpTimestamp(AH, "Started on", AH->createDate);
486
487	if (ropt->single_txn)
488	{
489		if (AH->connection)
490			StartTransaction(AHX);
491		else
492			ahprintf(AH, "BEGIN;\n\n");
493	}
494
495	/*
496	 * Establish important parameter values right away.
497	 */
498	_doSetFixedOutputState(AH);
499
500	AH->stage = STAGE_PROCESSING;
501
502	/*
503	 * Drop the items at the start, in reverse order
504	 */
505	if (ropt->dropSchema)
506	{
507		for (te = AH->toc->prev; te != AH->toc; te = te->prev)
508		{
509			AH->currentTE = te;
510
511			/*
512			 * In createDB mode, issue a DROP *only* for the database as a
513			 * whole.  Issuing drops against anything else would be wrong,
514			 * because at this point we're connected to the wrong database.
515			 * (The DATABASE PROPERTIES entry, if any, should be treated like
516			 * the DATABASE entry.)
517			 */
518			if (ropt->createDB)
519			{
520				if (strcmp(te->desc, "DATABASE") != 0 &&
521					strcmp(te->desc, "DATABASE PROPERTIES") != 0)
522					continue;
523			}
524
525			/* Otherwise, drop anything that's selected and has a dropStmt */
526			if (((te->reqs & (REQ_SCHEMA | REQ_DATA)) != 0) && te->dropStmt)
527			{
528				bool		not_allowed_in_txn = false;
529
530				pg_log_info("dropping %s %s", te->desc, te->tag);
531
532				/*
533				 * In --transaction-size mode, we have to temporarily exit our
534				 * transaction block to drop objects that can't be dropped
535				 * within a transaction.
536				 */
537				if (ropt->txn_size > 0)
538				{
539					if (strcmp(te->desc, "DATABASE") == 0 ||
540						strcmp(te->desc, "DATABASE PROPERTIES") == 0)
541					{
542						not_allowed_in_txn = true;
543						if (AH->connection)
544							CommitTransaction(AHX);
545						else
546							ahprintf(AH, "COMMIT;\n");
547					}
548				}
549
550				/* Select owner and schema as necessary */
551				_becomeOwner(AH, te);
552				_selectOutputSchema(AH, te->namespace);
553
554				/*
555				 * Now emit the DROP command, if the object has one.  Note we
556				 * don't necessarily emit it verbatim; at this point we add an
557				 * appropriate IF EXISTS clause, if the user requested it.
558				 */
559				if (strcmp(te->desc, "BLOB METADATA") == 0)
560				{
561					/* We must generate the per-blob commands */
562					if (ropt->if_exists)
563						IssueCommandPerBlob(AH, te,
564											"SELECT pg_catalog.lo_unlink(oid) "
565											"FROM pg_catalog.pg_largeobject_metadata "
566											"WHERE oid = '", "'");
567					else
568						IssueCommandPerBlob(AH, te,
569											"SELECT pg_catalog.lo_unlink('",
570											"')");
571				}
572				else if (*te->dropStmt != '\0')
573				{
574					if (!ropt->if_exists ||
575						strncmp(te->dropStmt, "--", 2) == 0)
576					{
577						/*
578						 * Without --if-exists, or if it's just a comment (as
579						 * happens for the public schema), print the dropStmt
580						 * as-is.
581						 */
582						ahprintf(AH, "%s", te->dropStmt);
583					}
584					else
585					{
586						/*
587						 * Inject an appropriate spelling of "if exists".  For
588						 * old-style large objects, we have a routine that
589						 * knows how to do it, without depending on
590						 * te->dropStmt; use that.  For other objects we need
591						 * to parse the command.
592						 */
593						if (strcmp(te->desc, "BLOB") == 0)
594						{
595							DropLOIfExists(AH, te->catalogId.oid);
596						}
597						else
598						{
599							char	   *dropStmt = pg_strdup(te->dropStmt);
600							char	   *dropStmtOrig = dropStmt;
601							PQExpBuffer ftStmt = createPQExpBuffer();
602
603							/*
604							 * Need to inject IF EXISTS clause after ALTER
605							 * TABLE part in ALTER TABLE .. DROP statement
606							 */
607							if (strncmp(dropStmt, "ALTER TABLE", 11) == 0)
608							{
609								appendPQExpBufferStr(ftStmt,
610													 "ALTER TABLE IF EXISTS");
611								dropStmt = dropStmt + 11;
612							}
613
614							/*
615							 * ALTER TABLE..ALTER COLUMN..DROP DEFAULT does
616							 * not support the IF EXISTS clause, and therefore
617							 * we simply emit the original command for DEFAULT
618							 * objects (modulo the adjustment made above).
619							 *
620							 * Likewise, don't mess with DATABASE PROPERTIES.
621							 *
622							 * If we used CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW as a means of
623							 * quasi-dropping an ON SELECT rule, that should
624							 * be emitted unchanged as well.
625							 *
626							 * For other object types, we need to extract the
627							 * first part of the DROP which includes the
628							 * object type.  Most of the time this matches
629							 * te->desc, so search for that; however for the
630							 * different kinds of CONSTRAINTs, we know to
631							 * search for hardcoded "DROP CONSTRAINT" instead.
632							 */
633							if (strcmp(te->desc, "DEFAULT") == 0 ||
634								strcmp(te->desc, "DATABASE PROPERTIES") == 0 ||
635								strncmp(dropStmt, "CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW", 22) == 0)
636								appendPQExpBufferStr(ftStmt, dropStmt);
637							else
638							{
639								char		buffer[40];
640								char	   *mark;
641
642								if (strcmp(te->desc, "CONSTRAINT") == 0 ||
643									strcmp(te->desc, "CHECK CONSTRAINT") == 0 ||
644									strcmp(te->desc, "FK CONSTRAINT") == 0)
645									strcpy(buffer, "DROP CONSTRAINT");
646								else
647									snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "DROP %s",
648											 te->desc);
649
650								mark = strstr(dropStmt, buffer);
651
652								if (mark)
653								{
654									*mark = '\0';
655									appendPQExpBuffer(ftStmt, "%s%s IF EXISTS%s",
656													  dropStmt, buffer,
657													  mark + strlen(buffer));
658								}
659								else
660								{
661									/* complain and emit unmodified command */
662									pg_log_warning("could not find where to insert IF EXISTS in statement \"%s\"",
663												   dropStmtOrig);
664									appendPQExpBufferStr(ftStmt, dropStmt);
665								}
666							}
667
668							ahprintf(AH, "%s", ftStmt->data);
669
670							destroyPQExpBuffer(ftStmt);
671							pg_free(dropStmtOrig);
672						}
673					}
674				}
675
676				/*
677				 * In --transaction-size mode, re-establish the transaction
678				 * block if needed; otherwise, commit after every N drops.
679				 */
680				if (ropt->txn_size > 0)
681				{
682					if (not_allowed_in_txn)
683					{
684						if (AH->connection)
685							StartTransaction(AHX);
686						else
687							ahprintf(AH, "BEGIN;\n");
688						AH->txnCount = 0;
689					}
690					else if (++AH->txnCount >= ropt->txn_size)
691					{
692						if (AH->connection)
693						{
694							CommitTransaction(AHX);
695							StartTransaction(AHX);
696						}
697						else
698							ahprintf(AH, "COMMIT;\nBEGIN;\n");
699						AH->txnCount = 0;
700					}
701				}
702			}
703		}
704
705		/*
706		 * _selectOutputSchema may have set currSchema to reflect the effect
707		 * of a "SET search_path" command it emitted.  However, by now we may
708		 * have dropped that schema; or it might not have existed in the first
709		 * place.  In either case the effective value of search_path will not
710		 * be what we think.  Forcibly reset currSchema so that we will
711		 * re-establish the search_path setting when needed (after creating
712		 * the schema).
713		 *
714		 * If we treated users as pg_dump'able objects then we'd need to reset
715		 * currUser here too.
716		 */
717		free(AH->currSchema);
718		AH->currSchema = NULL;
719	}
720
721	if (parallel_mode)
722	{
723		/*
724		 * In parallel mode, turn control over to the parallel-restore logic.
725		 */
726		ParallelState *pstate;
727		TocEntry	pending_list;
728
729		/* The archive format module may need some setup for this */
730		if (AH->PrepParallelRestorePtr)
731			AH->PrepParallelRestorePtr(AH);
732
733		pending_list_header_init(&pending_list);
734
735		/* This runs PRE_DATA items and then disconnects from the database */
736		restore_toc_entries_prefork(AH, &pending_list);
737		Assert(AH->connection == NULL);
738
739		/* ParallelBackupStart() will actually fork the processes */
740		pstate = ParallelBackupStart(AH);
741		restore_toc_entries_parallel(AH, pstate, &pending_list);
742		ParallelBackupEnd(AH, pstate);
743
744		/* reconnect the leader and see if we missed something */
745		restore_toc_entries_postfork(AH, &pending_list);
746		Assert(AH->connection != NULL);
747	}
748	else
749	{
750		/*
751		 * In serial mode, process everything in three phases: normal items,
752		 * then ACLs, then post-ACL items.  We might be able to skip one or
753		 * both extra phases in some cases, eg data-only restores.
754		 */
755		bool		haveACL = false;
756		bool		havePostACL = false;
757
758		for (te = AH->toc->next; te != AH->toc; te = te->next)
759		{
760			if ((te->reqs & (REQ_SCHEMA | REQ_DATA | REQ_STATS)) == 0)
761				continue;		/* ignore if not to be dumped at all */
762
763			switch (_tocEntryRestorePass(te))
764			{
765				case RESTORE_PASS_MAIN:
766					(void) restore_toc_entry(AH, te, false);
767					break;
768				case RESTORE_PASS_ACL:
769					haveACL = true;
770					break;
771				case RESTORE_PASS_POST_ACL:
772					havePostACL = true;
773					break;
774			}
775		}
776
777		if (haveACL)
778		{
779			for (te = AH->toc->next; te != AH->toc; te = te->next)
780			{
781				if ((te->reqs & (REQ_SCHEMA | REQ_DATA | REQ_STATS)) != 0 &&
782					_tocEntryRestorePass(te) == RESTORE_PASS_ACL)
783					(void) restore_toc_entry(AH, te, false);
784			}
785		}
786
787		if (havePostACL)
788		{
789			for (te = AH->toc->next; te != AH->toc; te = te->next)
790			{
791				if ((te->reqs & (REQ_SCHEMA | REQ_DATA | REQ_STATS)) != 0 &&
792					_tocEntryRestorePass(te) == RESTORE_PASS_POST_ACL)
793					(void) restore_toc_entry(AH, te, false);
794			}
795		}
796	}
797
798	/*
799	 * Close out any persistent transaction we may have.  While these two
800	 * cases are started in different places, we can end both cases here.
801	 */
802	if (ropt->single_txn || ropt->txn_size > 0)
803	{
804		if (AH->connection)
805			CommitTransaction(AHX);
806		else
807			ahprintf(AH, "COMMIT;\n\n");
808	}
809
810	if (AH->public.verbose)
811		dumpTimestamp(AH, "Completed on", time(NULL));
812
813	ahprintf(AH, "--\n-- PostgreSQL database dump complete\n--\n\n");
814
815	/*
816	 * If generating plain-text output, exit restricted mode at the very end
817	 * of the script. This is not pro forma; in particular, pg_dumpall
818	 * requires this when transitioning from one database to another.
819	 */
820	if (ropt->restrict_key)
821		ahprintf(AH, "\\unrestrict %s\n\n", ropt->restrict_key);
822
823	/*
824	 * Clean up & we're done.
825	 */
826	AH->stage = STAGE_FINALIZING;
827
828	if (ropt->filename || ropt->compression_spec.algorithm != PG_COMPRESSION_NONE)
829		RestoreOutput(AH, sav);
830
831	if (ropt->useDB)
832		DisconnectDatabase(&AH->public);
833}
834
835/*
836 * Restore a single TOC item.  Used in both parallel and non-parallel restore;
837 * is_parallel is true if we are in a worker child process.
838 *
839 * Returns 0 normally, but WORKER_CREATE_DONE or WORKER_INHIBIT_DATA if
840 * the parallel parent has to make the corresponding status update.
841 */
842static int
843restore_toc_entry(ArchiveHandle *AH, TocEntry *te, bool is_parallel)
844{
845	RestoreOptions *ropt = AH->public.ropt;
846	int			status = WORKER_OK;
847	int			reqs;
848	bool		defnDumped;
849
850	AH->currentTE = te;
851
852	/* Dump any relevant dump warnings to stderr */
853	if (!ropt->suppressDumpWarnings && strcmp(te->desc, "WARNING") == 0)
854	{
855		if (ropt->dumpSchema && te->defn != NULL && strlen(te->defn) != 0)
856			pg_log_warning("warning from original dump file: %s", te->defn);
857		else if (te->copyStmt != NULL && strlen(te->copyStmt) != 0)
858			pg_log_warning("warning from original dump file: %s", te->copyStmt);
859	}
860
861	/* Work out what, if anything, we want from this entry */
862	reqs = te->reqs;
863
864	defnDumped = false;
865
866	/*
867	 * If it has a schema component that we want, then process that
868	 */
869	if ((reqs & REQ_SCHEMA) != 0)
870	{
871		bool		object_is_db = false;
872
873		/*
874		 * In --transaction-size mode, must exit our transaction block to
875		 * create a database or set its properties.
876		 */
877		if (strcmp(te->desc, "DATABASE") == 0 ||
878			strcmp(te->desc, "DATABASE PROPERTIES") == 0)
879		{
880			object_is_db = true;
881			if (ropt->txn_size > 0)
882			{
883				if (AH->connection)
884					CommitTransaction(&AH->public);
885				else
886					ahprintf(AH, "COMMIT;\n\n");
887			}
888		}
889
890		/* Show namespace in log message if available */
891		if (te->namespace)
892			pg_log_info("creating %s \"%s.%s\"",
893						te->desc, te->namespace, te->tag);
894		else
895			pg_log_info("creating %s \"%s\"",
896						te->desc, te->tag);
897
898		_printTocEntry(AH, te, TOC_PREFIX_NONE);
899		defnDumped = true;
900
901		if (strcmp(te->desc, "TABLE") == 0)
902		{
903			if (AH->lastErrorTE == te)
904			{
905				/*
906				 * We failed to create the table. If
907				 * --no-data-for-failed-tables was given, mark the
908				 * corresponding TABLE DATA to be ignored.
909				 *
910				 * In the parallel case this must be done in the parent, so we
911				 * just set the return value.
912				 */
913				if (ropt->noDataForFailedTables)
914				{
915					if (is_parallel)
916						status = WORKER_INHIBIT_DATA;
917					else
918						inhibit_data_for_failed_table(AH, te);
919				}
920			}
921			else
922			{
923				/*
924				 * We created the table successfully.  Mark the corresponding
925				 * TABLE DATA for possible truncation.
926				 *
927				 * In the parallel case this must be done in the parent, so we
928				 * just set the return value.
929				 */
930				if (is_parallel)
931					status = WORKER_CREATE_DONE;
932				else
933					mark_create_done(AH, te);
934			}
935		}
936
937		/*
938		 * If we created a DB, connect to it.  Also, if we changed DB
939		 * properties, reconnect to ensure that relevant GUC settings are
940		 * applied to our session.  (That also restarts the transaction block
941		 * in --transaction-size mode.)
942		 */
943		if (object_is_db)
944		{
945			pg_log_info("connecting to new database \"%s\"", te->tag);
946			_reconnectToDB(AH, te->tag);
947		}
948	}
949
950	/*
951	 * If it has a data component that we want, then process that
952	 */
953	if ((reqs & REQ_DATA) != 0)
954	{
955		/*
956		 * hadDumper will be set if there is genuine data component for this
957		 * node. Otherwise, we need to check the defn field for statements
958		 * that need to be executed in data-only restores.
959		 */
960		if (te->hadDumper)
961		{
962			/*
963			 * If we can output the data, then restore it.
964			 */
965			if (AH->PrintTocDataPtr != NULL)
966			{
967				_printTocEntry(AH, te, TOC_PREFIX_DATA);
968
969				if (strcmp(te->desc, "BLOBS") == 0 ||
970					strcmp(te->desc, "BLOB COMMENTS") == 0)
971				{
972					pg_log_info("processing %s", te->desc);
973
974					_selectOutputSchema(AH, "pg_catalog");
975
976					/* Send BLOB COMMENTS data to ExecuteSimpleCommands() */
977					if (strcmp(te->desc, "BLOB COMMENTS") == 0)
978						AH->outputKind = OUTPUT_OTHERDATA;
979
980					AH->PrintTocDataPtr(AH, te);
981
982					AH->outputKind = OUTPUT_SQLCMDS;
983				}
984				else
985				{
986					bool		use_truncate;
987
988					_disableTriggersIfNecessary(AH, te);
989
990					/* Select owner and schema as necessary */
991					_becomeOwner(AH, te);
992					_selectOutputSchema(AH, te->namespace);
993
994					pg_log_info("processing data for table \"%s.%s\"",
995								te->namespace, te->tag);
996
997					/*
998					 * In parallel restore, if we created the table earlier in
999					 * this run (so that we know it is empty) and we are not
1000					 * restoring a load-via-partition-root data item then we
1001					 * wrap the COPY in a transaction and precede it with a
1002					 * TRUNCATE.  If wal_level is set to minimal this prevents
1003					 * WAL-logging the COPY.  This obtains a speedup similar
1004					 * to that from using single_txn mode in non-parallel
1005					 * restores.
1006					 *
1007					 * We mustn't do this for load-via-partition-root cases
1008					 * because some data might get moved across partition
1009					 * boundaries, risking deadlock and/or loss of previously
1010					 * loaded data.  (We assume that all partitions of a
1011					 * partitioned table will be treated the same way.)
1012					 */
1013					use_truncate = is_parallel && te->created &&
1014						!is_load_via_partition_root(te);
1015
1016					if (use_truncate)
1017					{
1018						/*
1019						 * Parallel restore is always talking directly to a
1020						 * server, so no need to see if we should issue BEGIN.
1021						 */
1022						StartTransaction(&AH->public);
1023
1024						/*
1025						 * Issue TRUNCATE with ONLY so that child tables are
1026						 * not wiped.
1027						 */
1028						ahprintf(AH, "TRUNCATE TABLE ONLY %s;\n\n",
1029								 fmtQualifiedId(te->namespace, te->tag));
1030					}
1031
1032					/*
1033					 * If we have a copy statement, use it.
1034					 */
1035					if (te->copyStmt && strlen(te->copyStmt) > 0)
1036					{
1037						ahprintf(AH, "%s", te->copyStmt);
1038						AH->outputKind = OUTPUT_COPYDATA;
1039					}
1040					else
1041						AH->outputKind = OUTPUT_OTHERDATA;
1042
1043					AH->PrintTocDataPtr(AH, te);
1044
1045					/*
1046					 * Terminate COPY if needed.
1047					 */
1048					if (AH->outputKind == OUTPUT_COPYDATA &&
1049						RestoringToDB(AH))
1050						EndDBCopyMode(&AH->public, te->tag);
1051					AH->outputKind = OUTPUT_SQLCMDS;
1052
1053					/* close out the transaction started above */
1054					if (use_truncate)
1055						CommitTransaction(&AH->public);
1056
1057					_enableTriggersIfNecessary(AH, te);
1058				}
1059			}
1060		}
1061		else if (!defnDumped)
1062		{
1063			/* If we haven't already dumped the defn part, do so now */
1064			pg_log_info("executing %s %s", te->desc, te->tag);
1065			_printTocEntry(AH, te, TOC_PREFIX_NONE);
1066		}
1067	}
1068
1069	/*
1070	 * If it has a statistics component that we want, then process that
1071	 */
1072	if ((reqs & REQ_STATS) != 0)
1073		_printTocEntry(AH, te, TOC_PREFIX_STATS);
1074
1075	/*
1076	 * If we emitted anything for this TOC entry, that counts as one action
1077	 * against the transaction-size limit.  Commit if it's time to.
1078	 */
1079	if ((reqs & (REQ_SCHEMA | REQ_DATA | REQ_STATS)) != 0 && ropt->txn_size > 0)
1080	{
1081		if (++AH->txnCount >= ropt->txn_size)
1082		{
1083			if (AH->connection)
1084			{
1085				CommitTransaction(&AH->public);
1086				StartTransaction(&AH->public);
1087			}
1088			else
1089				ahprintf(AH, "COMMIT;\nBEGIN;\n\n");
1090			AH->txnCount = 0;
1091		}
1092	}
1093
1094	if (AH->public.n_errors > 0 && status == WORKER_OK)
1095		status = WORKER_IGNORED_ERRORS;
1096
1097	return status;
1098}
1099
1100/*
1101 * Allocate a new RestoreOptions block.
1102 * This is mainly so we can initialize it, but also for future expansion,
1103 */
1104RestoreOptions *
1105NewRestoreOptions(void)
1106{
1107	RestoreOptions *opts;
1108
1109	opts = (RestoreOptions *) pg_malloc0(sizeof(RestoreOptions));
1110
1111	/* set any fields that shouldn't default to zeroes */
1112	opts->format = archUnknown;
1113	opts->cparams.promptPassword = TRI_DEFAULT;
1114	opts->dumpSections = DUMP_UNSECTIONED;
1115	opts->compression_spec.algorithm = PG_COMPRESSION_NONE;
1116	opts->compression_spec.level = 0;
1117	opts->dumpSchema = true;
1118	opts->dumpData = true;
1119	opts->dumpStatistics = true;
1120
1121	return opts;
1122}
1123
1124static void
1125_disableTriggersIfNecessary(ArchiveHandle *AH, TocEntry *te)
1126{
1127	RestoreOptions *ropt = AH->public.ropt;
1128
1129	/* This hack is only needed in a data-only restore */
1130	if (ropt->dumpSchema || !ropt->disable_triggers)
1131		return;
1132
1133	pg_log_info("disabling triggers for %s", te->tag);
1134
1135	/*
1136	 * Become superuser if possible, since they are the only ones who can
1137	 * disable constraint triggers.  If -S was not given, assume the initial
1138	 * user identity is a superuser.  (XXX would it be better to become the
1139	 * table owner?)
1140	 */
1141	_becomeUser(AH, ropt->superuser);
1142
1143	/*
1144	 * Disable them.
1145	 */
1146	ahprintf(AH, "ALTER TABLE %s DISABLE TRIGGER ALL;\n\n",
1147			 fmtQualifiedId(te->namespace, te->tag));
1148}
1149
1150static void
1151_enableTriggersIfNecessary(ArchiveHandle *AH, TocEntry *te)
1152{
1153	RestoreOptions *ropt = AH->public.ropt;
1154
1155	/* This hack is only needed in a data-only restore */
1156	if (ropt->dumpSchema || !ropt->disable_triggers)
1157		return;
1158
1159	pg_log_info("enabling triggers for %s", te->tag);
1160
1161	/*
1162	 * Become superuser if possible, since they are the only ones who can
1163	 * disable constraint triggers.  If -S was not given, assume the initial
1164	 * user identity is a superuser.  (XXX would it be better to become the
1165	 * table owner?)
1166	 */
1167	_becomeUser(AH, ropt->superuser);
1168
1169	/*
1170	 * Enable them.
1171	 */
1172	ahprintf(AH, "ALTER TABLE %s ENABLE TRIGGER ALL;\n\n",
1173			 fmtQualifiedId(te->namespace, te->tag));
1174}
1175
1176/*
1177 * Detect whether a TABLE DATA TOC item is performing "load via partition
1178 * root", that is the target table is an ancestor partition rather than the
1179 * table the TOC item is nominally for.
1180 *
1181 * In newer archive files this can be detected by checking for a special
1182 * comment placed in te->defn.  In older files we have to fall back to seeing
1183 * if the COPY statement targets the named table or some other one.  This
1184 * will not work for data dumped as INSERT commands, so we could give a false
1185 * negative in that case; fortunately, that's a rarely-used option.
1186 */
1187static bool
1188is_load_via_partition_root(TocEntry *te)
1189{
1190	if (te->defn &&
1191		strncmp(te->defn, "-- load via partition root ", 27) == 0)
1192		return true;
1193	if (te->copyStmt && *te->copyStmt)
1194	{
1195		PQExpBuffer copyStmt = createPQExpBuffer();
1196		bool		result;
1197
1198		/*
1199		 * Build the initial part of the COPY as it would appear if the
1200		 * nominal target table is the actual target.  If we see anything
1201		 * else, it must be a load-via-partition-root case.
1202		 */
1203		appendPQExpBuffer(copyStmt, "COPY %s ",
1204						  fmtQualifiedId(te->namespace, te->tag));
1205		result = strncmp(te->copyStmt, copyStmt->data, copyStmt->len) != 0;
1206		destroyPQExpBuffer(copyStmt);
1207		return result;
1208	}
1209	/* Assume it's not load-via-partition-root */
1210	return false;
1211}
1212
1213/*
1214 * This is a routine that is part of the dumper interface, hence the 'Archive*' parameter.
1215 */
1216
1217/* Public */
1218void
1219WriteData(Archive *AHX, const void *data, size_t dLen)
1220{
1221	ArchiveHandle *AH = (ArchiveHandle *) AHX;
1222
1223	if (!AH->currToc)
1224		pg_fatal("internal error -- WriteData cannot be called outside the context of a DataDumper routine");
1225
1226	AH->WriteDataPtr(AH, data, dLen);
1227}
1228
1229/*
1230 * Create a new TOC entry. The TOC was designed as a TOC, but is now the
1231 * repository for all metadata. But the name has stuck.
1232 *
1233 * The new entry is added to the Archive's TOC list.  Most callers can ignore
1234 * the result value because nothing else need be done, but a few want to
1235 * manipulate the TOC entry further.
1236 */
1237
1238/* Public */
1239TocEntry *
1240ArchiveEntry(Archive *AHX, CatalogId catalogId, DumpId dumpId,
1241			 ArchiveOpts *opts)
1242{
1243	ArchiveHandle *AH = (ArchiveHandle *) AHX;
1244	TocEntry   *newToc;
1245
1246	newToc = (TocEntry *) pg_malloc0(sizeof(TocEntry));
1247
1248	AH->tocCount++;
1249	if (dumpId > AH->maxDumpId)
1250		AH->maxDumpId = dumpId;
1251
1252	newToc->prev = AH->toc->prev;
1253	newToc->next = AH->toc;
1254	AH->toc->prev->next = newToc;
1255	AH->toc->prev = newToc;
1256
1257	newToc->catalogId = catalogId;
1258	newToc->dumpId = dumpId;
1259	newToc->section = opts->section;
1260
1261	newToc->tag = pg_strdup(opts->tag);
1262	newToc->namespace = opts->namespace ? pg_strdup(opts->namespace) : NULL;
1263	newToc->tablespace = opts->tablespace ? pg_strdup(opts->tablespace) : NULL;
1264	newToc->tableam = opts->tableam ? pg_strdup(opts->tableam) : NULL;
1265	newToc->relkind = opts->relkind;
1266	newToc->owner = opts->owner ? pg_strdup(opts->owner) : NULL;
1267	newToc->desc = pg_strdup(opts->description);
1268	newToc->defn = opts->createStmt ? pg_strdup(opts->createStmt) : NULL;
1269	newToc->dropStmt = opts->dropStmt ? pg_strdup(opts->dropStmt) : NULL;
1270	newToc->copyStmt = opts->copyStmt ? pg_strdup(opts->copyStmt) : NULL;
1271
1272	if (opts->nDeps > 0)
1273	{
1274		newToc->dependencies = (DumpId *) pg_malloc(opts->nDeps * sizeof(DumpId));
1275		memcpy(newToc->dependencies, opts->deps, opts->nDeps * sizeof(DumpId));
1276		newToc->nDeps = opts->nDeps;
1277	}
1278	else
1279	{
1280		newToc->dependencies = NULL;
1281		newToc->nDeps = 0;
1282	}
1283
1284	newToc->dataDumper = opts->dumpFn;
1285	newToc->dataDumperArg = opts->dumpArg;
1286	newToc->hadDumper = opts->dumpFn ? true : false;
1287
1288	newToc->defnDumper = opts->defnFn;
1289	newToc->defnDumperArg = opts->defnArg;
1290
1291	newToc->formatData = NULL;
1292	newToc->dataLength = 0;
1293
1294	if (AH->ArchiveEntryPtr != NULL)
1295		AH->ArchiveEntryPtr(AH, newToc);
1296
1297	return newToc;
1298}
1299
1300/* Public */
1301void
1302PrintTOCSummary(Archive *AHX)
1303{
1304	ArchiveHandle *AH = (ArchiveHandle *) AHX;
1305	RestoreOptions *ropt = AH->public.ropt;
1306	TocEntry   *te;
1307	pg_compress_specification out_compression_spec = {0};
1308	teSection	curSection;
1309	CompressFileHandle *sav;
1310	const char *fmtName;
1311	char		stamp_str[64];
1312
1313	/* TOC is always uncompressed */
1314	out_compression_spec.algorithm = PG_COMPRESSION_NONE;
1315
1316	sav = SaveOutput(AH);
1317	if (ropt->filename)
1318		SetOutput(AH, ropt->filename, out_compression_spec);
1319
1320	if (strftime(stamp_str, sizeof(stamp_str), PGDUMP_STRFTIME_FMT,
1321				 localtime(&AH->createDate)) == 0)
1322		strcpy(stamp_str, "[unknown]");
1323
1324	ahprintf(AH, ";\n; Archive created at %s\n", stamp_str);
1325	ahprintf(AH, ";     dbname: %s\n;     TOC Entries: %d\n;     Compression: %s\n",
1326			 sanitize_line(AH->archdbname, false),
1327			 AH->tocCount,
1328			 get_compress_algorithm_name(AH->compression_spec.algorithm));
1329
1330	switch (AH->format)
1331	{
1332		case archCustom:
1333			fmtName = "CUSTOM";
1334			break;
1335		case archDirectory:
1336			fmtName = "DIRECTORY";
1337			break;
1338		case archTar:
1339			fmtName = "TAR";
1340			break;
1341		default:
1342			fmtName = "UNKNOWN";
1343	}
1344
1345	ahprintf(AH, ";     Dump Version: %d.%d-%d\n",
1346			 ARCHIVE_MAJOR(AH->version), ARCHIVE_MINOR(AH->version), ARCHIVE_REV(AH->version));
1347	ahprintf(AH, ";     Format: %s\n", fmtName);
1348	ahprintf(AH, ";     Integer: %d bytes\n", (int) AH->intSize);
1349	ahprintf(AH, ";     Offset: %d bytes\n", (int) AH->offSize);
1350	if (AH->archiveRemoteVersion)
1351		ahprintf(AH, ";     Dumped from database version: %s\n",
1352				 AH->archiveRemoteVersion);
1353	if (AH->archiveDumpVersion)
1354		ahprintf(AH, ";     Dumped by pg_dump version: %s\n",
1355				 AH->archiveDumpVersion);
1356
1357	ahprintf(AH, ";\n;\n; Selected TOC Entries:\n;\n");
1358
1359	curSection = SECTION_PRE_DATA;
1360	for (te = AH->toc->next; te != AH->toc; te = te->next)
1361	{
1362		/* This bit must match ProcessArchiveRestoreOptions' marking logic */
1363		if (te->section != SECTION_NONE)
1364			curSection = te->section;
1365		te->reqs = _tocEntryRequired(te, curSection, AH);
1366		/* Now, should we print it? */
1367		if (ropt->verbose ||
1368			(te->reqs & (REQ_SCHEMA | REQ_DATA | REQ_STATS)) != 0)
1369		{
1370			char	   *sanitized_name;
1371			char	   *sanitized_schema;
1372			char	   *sanitized_owner;
1373
1374			/*
1375			 */
1376			sanitized_name = sanitize_line(te->tag, false);
1377			sanitized_schema = sanitize_line(te->namespace, true);
1378			sanitized_owner = sanitize_line(te->owner, false);
1379
1380			ahprintf(AH, "%d; %u %u %s %s %s %s\n", te->dumpId,
1381					 te->catalogId.tableoid, te->catalogId.oid,
1382					 te->desc, sanitized_schema, sanitized_name,
1383					 sanitized_owner);
1384
1385			free(sanitized_name);
1386			free(sanitized_schema);
1387			free(sanitized_owner);
1388		}
1389		if (ropt->verbose && te->nDeps > 0)
1390		{
1391			int			i;
1392
1393			ahprintf(AH, ";\tdepends on:");
1394			for (i = 0; i < te->nDeps; i++)
1395				ahprintf(AH, " %d", te->dependencies[i]);
1396			ahprintf(AH, "\n");
1397		}
1398	}
1399
1400	/* Enforce strict names checking */
1401	if (ropt->strict_names)
1402		StrictNamesCheck(ropt);
1403
1404	if (ropt->filename)
1405		RestoreOutput(AH, sav);
1406}
1407
1408/***********
1409 * Large Object Archival
1410 ***********/
1411
1412/* Called by a dumper to signal start of a LO */
1413int
1414StartLO(Archive *AHX, Oid oid)
1415{
1416	ArchiveHandle *AH = (ArchiveHandle *) AHX;
1417
1418	if (!AH->StartLOPtr)
1419		pg_fatal("large-object output not supported in chosen format");
1420
1421	AH->StartLOPtr(AH, AH->currToc, oid);
1422
1423	return 1;
1424}
1425
1426/* Called by a dumper to signal end of a LO */
1427int
1428EndLO(Archive *AHX, Oid oid)
1429{
1430	ArchiveHandle *AH = (ArchiveHandle *) AHX;
1431
1432	if (AH->EndLOPtr)
1433		AH->EndLOPtr(AH, AH->currToc, oid);
1434
1435	return 1;
1436}
1437
1438/**********
1439 * Large Object Restoration
1440 **********/
1441
1442/*
1443 * Called by a format handler before a group of LOs is restored
1444 */
1445void
1446StartRestoreLOs(ArchiveHandle *AH)
1447{
1448	RestoreOptions *ropt = AH->public.ropt;
1449
1450	/*
1451	 * LOs must be restored within a transaction block, since we need the LO
1452	 * handle to stay open while we write it.  Establish a transaction unless
1453	 * there's one being used globally.
1454	 */
1455	if (!(ropt->single_txn || ropt->txn_size > 0))
1456	{
1457		if (AH->connection)
1458			StartTransaction(&AH->public);
1459		else
1460			ahprintf(AH, "BEGIN;\n\n");
1461	}
1462
1463	AH->loCount = 0;
1464}
1465
1466/*
1467 * Called by a format handler after a group of LOs is restored
1468 */
1469void
1470EndRestoreLOs(ArchiveHandle *AH)
1471{
1472	RestoreOptions *ropt = AH->public.ropt;
1473
1474	if (!(ropt->single_txn || ropt->txn_size > 0))
1475	{
1476		if (AH->connection)
1477			CommitTransaction(&AH->public);
1478		else
1479			ahprintf(AH, "COMMIT;\n\n");
1480	}
1481
1482	pg_log_info(ngettext("restored %d large object",
1483						 "restored %d large objects",
1484						 AH->loCount),
1485				AH->loCount);
1486}
1487
1488
1489/*
1490 * Called by a format handler to initiate restoration of a LO
1491 */
1492void
1493StartRestoreLO(ArchiveHandle *AH, Oid oid, bool drop)
1494{
1495	bool		old_lo_style = (AH->version < K_VERS_1_12);
1496	Oid			loOid;
1497
1498	AH->loCount++;
1499
1500	/* Initialize the LO Buffer */
1501	if (AH->lo_buf == NULL)
1502	{
1503		/* First time through (in this process) so allocate the buffer */
1504		AH->lo_buf_size = LOBBUFSIZE;
1505		AH->lo_buf = pg_malloc(LOBBUFSIZE);
1506	}
1507	AH->lo_buf_used = 0;
1508
1509	pg_log_info("restoring large object with OID %u", oid);
1510
1511	/* With an old archive we must do drop and create logic here */
1512	if (old_lo_style && drop)
1513		DropLOIfExists(AH, oid);
1514
1515	if (AH->connection)
1516	{
1517		if (old_lo_style)
1518		{
1519			loOid = lo_create(AH->connection, oid);
1520			if (loOid == 0 || loOid != oid)
1521				pg_fatal("could not create large object %u: %s",
1522						 oid, PQerrorMessage(AH->connection));
1523		}
1524		AH->loFd = lo_open(AH->connection, oid, INV_WRITE);
1525		if (AH->loFd == -1)
1526			pg_fatal("could not open large object %u: %s",
1527					 oid, PQerrorMessage(AH->connection));
1528	}
1529	else
1530	{
1531		if (old_lo_style)
1532			ahprintf(AH, "SELECT pg_catalog.lo_open(pg_catalog.lo_create('%u'), %d);\n",
1533					 oid, INV_WRITE);
1534		else
1535			ahprintf(AH, "SELECT pg_catalog.lo_open('%u', %d);\n",
1536					 oid, INV_WRITE);
1537	}
1538
1539	AH->writingLO = true;
1540}
1541
1542void
1543EndRestoreLO(ArchiveHandle *AH, Oid oid)
1544{
1545	if (AH->lo_buf_used > 0)
1546	{
1547		/* Write remaining bytes from the LO buffer */
1548		dump_lo_buf(AH);
1549	}
1550
1551	AH->writingLO = false;
1552
1553	if (AH->connection)
1554	{
1555		lo_close(AH->connection, AH->loFd);
1556		AH->loFd = -1;
1557	}
1558	else
1559	{
1560		ahprintf(AH, "SELECT pg_catalog.lo_close(0);\n\n");
1561	}
1562}
1563
1564/***********
1565 * Sorting and Reordering
1566 ***********/
1567
1568void
1569SortTocFromFile(Archive *AHX)
1570{
1571	ArchiveHandle *AH = (ArchiveHandle *) AHX;
1572	RestoreOptions *ropt = AH->public.ropt;
1573	FILE	   *fh;
1574	StringInfoData linebuf;
1575
1576	/* Allocate space for the 'wanted' array, and init it */
1577	ropt->idWanted = (bool *) pg_malloc0(sizeof(bool) * AH->maxDumpId);
1578
1579	/* Setup the file */
1580	fh = fopen(ropt->tocFile, PG_BINARY_R);
1581	if (!fh)
1582		pg_fatal("could not open TOC file \"%s\": %m", ropt->tocFile);
1583
1584	initStringInfo(&linebuf);
1585
1586	while (pg_get_line_buf(fh, &linebuf))
1587	{
1588		char	   *cmnt;
1589		char	   *endptr;
1590		DumpId		id;
1591		TocEntry   *te;
1592
1593		/* Truncate line at comment, if any */
1594		cmnt = strchr(linebuf.data, ';');
1595		if (cmnt != NULL)
1596		{
1597			cmnt[0] = '\0';
1598			linebuf.len = cmnt - linebuf.data;
1599		}
1600
1601		/* Ignore if all blank */
1602		if (strspn(linebuf.data, " \t\r\n") == linebuf.len)
1603			continue;
1604
1605		/* Get an ID, check it's valid and not already seen */
1606		id = strtol(linebuf.data, &endptr, 10);
1607		if (endptr == linebuf.data || id <= 0 || id > AH->maxDumpId ||
1608			ropt->idWanted[id - 1])
1609		{
1610			pg_log_warning("line ignored: %s", linebuf.data);
1611			continue;
1612		}
1613
1614		/* Find TOC entry */
1615		te = getTocEntryByDumpId(AH, id);
1616		if (!te)
1617			pg_fatal("could not find entry for ID %d",
1618					 id);
1619
1620		/* Mark it wanted */
1621		ropt->idWanted[id - 1] = true;
1622
1623		/*
1624		 * Move each item to the end of the list as it is selected, so that
1625		 * they are placed in the desired order.  Any unwanted items will end
1626		 * up at the front of the list, which may seem unintuitive but it's
1627		 * what we need.  In an ordinary serial restore that makes no
1628		 * difference, but in a parallel restore we need to mark unrestored
1629		 * items' dependencies as satisfied before we start examining
1630		 * restorable items.  Otherwise they could have surprising
1631		 * side-effects on the order in which restorable items actually get
1632		 * restored.
1633		 */
1634		_moveBefore(AH->toc, te);
1635	}
1636
1637	pg_free(linebuf.data);
1638
1639	if (fclose(fh) != 0)
1640		pg_fatal("could not close TOC file: %m");
1641}
1642
1643/**********************
1644 * Convenience functions that look like standard IO functions
1645 * for writing data when in dump mode.
1646 **********************/
1647
1648/* Public */
1649void
1650archputs(const char *s, Archive *AH)
1651{
1652	WriteData(AH, s, strlen(s));
1653}
1654
1655/* Public */
1656int
1657archprintf(Archive *AH, const char *fmt,...)
1658{
1659	int			save_errno = errno;
1660	char	   *p;
1661	size_t		len = 128;		/* initial assumption about buffer size */
1662	size_t		cnt;
1663
1664	for (;;)
1665	{
1666		va_list		args;
1667
1668		/* Allocate work buffer. */
1669		p = (char *) pg_malloc(len);
1670
1671		/* Try to format the data. */
1672		errno = save_errno;
1673		va_start(args, fmt);
1674		cnt = pvsnprintf(p, len, fmt, args);
1675		va_end(args);
1676
1677		if (cnt < len)
1678			break;				/* success */
1679
1680		/* Release buffer and loop around to try again with larger len. */
1681		free(p);
1682		len = cnt;
1683	}
1684
1685	WriteData(AH, p, cnt);
1686	free(p);
1687	return (int) cnt;
1688}
1689
1690
1691/*******************************
1692 * Stuff below here should be 'private' to the archiver routines
1693 *******************************/
1694
1695static void
1696SetOutput(ArchiveHandle *AH, const char *filename,
1697		  const pg_compress_specification compression_spec)
1698{
1699	CompressFileHandle *CFH;
1700	const char *mode;
1701	int			fn = -1;
1702
1703	if (filename)
1704	{
1705		if (strcmp(filename, "-") == 0)
1706			fn = fileno(stdout);
1707	}
1708	else if (AH->FH)
1709		fn = fileno(AH->FH);
1710	else if (AH->fSpec)
1711	{
1712		filename = AH->fSpec;
1713	}
1714	else
1715		fn = fileno(stdout);
1716
1717	if (AH->mode == archModeAppend)
1718		mode = PG_BINARY_A;
1719	else
1720		mode = PG_BINARY_W;
1721
1722	CFH = InitCompressFileHandle(compression_spec);
1723
1724	if (!CFH->open_func(filename, fn, mode, CFH))
1725	{
1726		if (filename)
1727			pg_fatal("could not open output file \"%s\": %m", filename);
1728		else
1729			pg_fatal("could not open output file: %m");
1730	}
1731
1732	AH->OF = CFH;
1733}
1734
1735static CompressFileHandle *
1736SaveOutput(ArchiveHandle *AH)
1737{
1738	return (CompressFileHandle *) AH->OF;
1739}
1740
1741static void
1742RestoreOutput(ArchiveHandle *AH, CompressFileHandle *savedOutput)
1743{
1744	errno = 0;
1745	if (!EndCompressFileHandle(AH->OF))
1746		pg_fatal("could not close output file: %m");
1747
1748	AH->OF = savedOutput;
1749}
1750
1751
1752
1753/*
1754 *	Print formatted text to the output file (usually stdout).
1755 */
1756int
1757ahprintf(ArchiveHandle *AH, const char *fmt,...)
1758{
1759	int			save_errno = errno;
1760	char	   *p;
1761	size_t		len = 128;		/* initial assumption about buffer size */
1762	size_t		cnt;
1763
1764	for (;;)
1765	{
1766		va_list		args;
1767
1768		/* Allocate work buffer. */
1769		p = (char *) pg_malloc(len);
1770
1771		/* Try to format the data. */
1772		errno = save_errno;
1773		va_start(args, fmt);
1774		cnt = pvsnprintf(p, len, fmt, args);
1775		va_end(args);
1776
1777		if (cnt < len)
1778			break;				/* success */
1779
1780		/* Release buffer and loop around to try again with larger len. */
1781		free(p);
1782		len = cnt;
1783	}
1784
1785	ahwrite(p, 1, cnt, AH);
1786	free(p);
1787	return (int) cnt;
1788}
1789
1790/*
1791 * Single place for logic which says 'We are restoring to a direct DB connection'.
1792 */
1793static int
1794RestoringToDB(ArchiveHandle *AH)
1795{
1796	RestoreOptions *ropt = AH->public.ropt;
1797
1798	return (ropt && ropt->useDB && AH->connection);
1799}
1800
1801/*
1802 * Dump the current contents of the LO data buffer while writing a LO
1803 */
1804static void
1805dump_lo_buf(ArchiveHandle *AH)
1806{
1807	if (AH->connection)
1808	{
1809		int			res;
1810
1811		res = lo_write(AH->connection, AH->loFd, AH->lo_buf, AH->lo_buf_used);
1812		pg_log_debug(ngettext("wrote %zu byte of large object data (result = %d)",
1813							  "wrote %zu bytes of large object data (result = %d)",
1814							  AH->lo_buf_used),
1815					 AH->lo_buf_used, res);
1816		/* We assume there are no short writes, only errors */
1817		if (res != AH->lo_buf_used)
1818			warn_or_exit_horribly(AH, "could not write to large object: %s",
1819								  PQerrorMessage(AH->connection));
1820	}
1821	else
1822	{
1823		PQExpBuffer buf = createPQExpBuffer();
1824
1825		appendByteaLiteralAHX(buf,
1826							  (const unsigned char *) AH->lo_buf,
1827							  AH->lo_buf_used,
1828							  AH);
1829
1830		/* Hack: turn off writingLO so ahwrite doesn't recurse to here */
1831		AH->writingLO = false;
1832		ahprintf(AH, "SELECT pg_catalog.lowrite(0, %s);\n", buf->data);
1833		AH->writingLO = true;
1834
1835		destroyPQExpBuffer(buf);
1836	}
1837	AH->lo_buf_used = 0;
1838}
1839
1840
1841/*
1842 *	Write buffer to the output file (usually stdout). This is used for
1843 *	outputting 'restore' scripts etc. It is even possible for an archive
1844 *	format to create a custom output routine to 'fake' a restore if it
1845 *	wants to generate a script (see TAR output).
1846 */
1847void
1848ahwrite(const void *ptr, size_t size, size_t nmemb, ArchiveHandle *AH)
1849{
1850	int			bytes_written = 0;
1851
1852	if (AH->writingLO)
1853	{
1854		size_t		remaining = size * nmemb;
1855
1856		while (AH->lo_buf_used + remaining > AH->lo_buf_size)
1857		{
1858			size_t		avail = AH->lo_buf_size - AH->lo_buf_used;
1859
1860			memcpy((char *) AH->lo_buf + AH->lo_buf_used, ptr, avail);
1861			ptr = (const char *) ptr + avail;
1862			remaining -= avail;
1863			AH->lo_buf_used += avail;
1864			dump_lo_buf(AH);
1865		}
1866
1867		memcpy((char *) AH->lo_buf + AH->lo_buf_used, ptr, remaining);
1868		AH->lo_buf_used += remaining;
1869
1870		bytes_written = size * nmemb;
1871	}
1872	else if (AH->CustomOutPtr)
1873		bytes_written = AH->CustomOutPtr(AH, ptr, size * nmemb);
1874
1875	/*
1876	 * If we're doing a restore, and it's direct to DB, and we're connected
1877	 * then send it to the DB.
1878	 */
1879	else if (RestoringToDB(AH))
1880		bytes_written = ExecuteSqlCommandBuf(&AH->public, (const char *) ptr, size * nmemb);
1881	else
1882	{
1883		CompressFileHandle *CFH = (CompressFileHandle *) AH->OF;
1884
1885		CFH->write_func(ptr, size * nmemb, CFH);
1886		bytes_written = size * nmemb;
1887	}
1888
1889	if (bytes_written != size * nmemb)
1890		WRITE_ERROR_EXIT;
1891}
1892
1893/* on some error, we may decide to go on... */
1894void
1895warn_or_exit_horribly(ArchiveHandle *AH, const char *fmt,...)
1896{
1897	va_list		ap;
1898
1899	switch (AH->stage)
1900	{
1901
1902		case STAGE_NONE:
1903			/* Do nothing special */
1904			break;
1905
1906		case STAGE_INITIALIZING:
1907			if (AH->stage != AH->lastErrorStage)
1908				pg_log_info("while INITIALIZING:");
1909			break;
1910
1911		case STAGE_PROCESSING:
1912			if (AH->stage != AH->lastErrorStage)
1913				pg_log_info("while PROCESSING TOC:");
1914			break;
1915
1916		case STAGE_FINALIZING:
1917			if (AH->stage != AH->lastErrorStage)
1918				pg_log_info("while FINALIZING:");
1919			break;
1920	}
1921	if (AH->currentTE != NULL && AH->currentTE != AH->lastErrorTE)
1922	{
1923		pg_log_info("from TOC entry %d; %u %u %s %s %s",
1924					AH->currentTE->dumpId,
1925					AH->currentTE->catalogId.tableoid,
1926					AH->currentTE->catalogId.oid,
1927					AH->currentTE->desc ? AH->currentTE->desc : "(no desc)",
1928					AH->currentTE->tag ? AH->currentTE->tag : "(no tag)",
1929					AH->currentTE->owner ? AH->currentTE->owner : "(no owner)");
1930	}
1931	AH->lastErrorStage = AH->stage;
1932	AH->lastErrorTE = AH->currentTE;
1933
1934	va_start(ap, fmt);
1935	pg_log_generic_v(PG_LOG_ERROR, PG_LOG_PRIMARY, fmt, ap);
1936	va_end(ap);
1937
1938	if (AH->public.exit_on_error)
1939		exit_nicely(1);
1940	else
1941		AH->public.n_errors++;
1942}
1943
1944#ifdef NOT_USED
1945
1946static void
1947_moveAfter(ArchiveHandle *AH, TocEntry *pos, TocEntry *te)
1948{
1949	/* Unlink te from list */
1950	te->prev->next = te->next;
1951	te->next->prev = te->prev;
1952
1953	/* and insert it after "pos" */
1954	te->prev = pos;
1955	te->next = pos->next;
1956	pos->next->prev = te;
1957	pos->next = te;
1958}
1959#endif
1960
1961static void
1962_moveBefore(TocEntry *pos, TocEntry *te)
1963{
1964	/* Unlink te from list */
1965	te->prev->next = te->next;
1966	te->next->prev = te->prev;
1967
1968	/* and insert it before "pos" */
1969	te->prev = pos->prev;
1970	te->next = pos;
1971	pos->prev->next = te;
1972	pos->prev = te;
1973}
1974
1975/*
1976 * Build index arrays for the TOC list
1977 *
1978 * This should be invoked only after we have created or read in all the TOC
1979 * items.
1980 *
1981 * The arrays are indexed by dump ID (so entry zero is unused).  Note that the
1982 * array entries run only up to maxDumpId.  We might see dependency dump IDs
1983 * beyond that (if the dump was partial); so always check the array bound
1984 * before trying to touch an array entry.
1985 */
1986static void
1987buildTocEntryArrays(ArchiveHandle *AH)
1988{
1989	DumpId		maxDumpId = AH->maxDumpId;
1990	TocEntry   *te;
1991
1992	AH->tocsByDumpId = (TocEntry **) pg_malloc0((maxDumpId + 1) * sizeof(TocEntry *));
1993	AH->tableDataId = (DumpId *) pg_malloc0((maxDumpId + 1) * sizeof(DumpId));
1994
1995	for (te = AH->toc->next; te != AH->toc; te = te->next)
1996	{
1997		/* this check is purely paranoia, maxDumpId should be correct */
1998		if (te->dumpId <= 0 || te->dumpId > maxDumpId)
1999			pg_fatal("bad dumpId");
2000
2001		/* tocsByDumpId indexes all TOCs by their dump ID */
2002		AH->tocsByDumpId[te->dumpId] = te;
2003
2004		/*
2005		 * tableDataId provides the TABLE DATA item's dump ID for each TABLE
2006		 * TOC entry that has a DATA item.  We compute this by reversing the
2007		 * TABLE DATA item's dependency, knowing that a TABLE DATA item has
2008		 * just one dependency and it is the TABLE item.
2009		 */
2010		if (strcmp(te->desc, "TABLE DATA") == 0 && te->nDeps > 0)
2011		{
2012			DumpId		tableId = te->dependencies[0];
2013
2014			/*
2015			 * The TABLE item might not have been in the archive, if this was
2016			 * a data-only dump; but its dump ID should be less than its data
2017			 * item's dump ID, so there should be a place for it in the array.
2018			 */
2019			if (tableId <= 0 || tableId > maxDumpId)
2020				pg_fatal("bad table dumpId for TABLE DATA item");
2021
2022			AH->tableDataId[tableId] = te->dumpId;
2023		}
2024	}
2025}
2026
2027TocEntry *
2028getTocEntryByDumpId(ArchiveHandle *AH, DumpId id)
2029{
2030	/* build index arrays if we didn't already */
2031	if (AH->tocsByDumpId == NULL)
2032		buildTocEntryArrays(AH);
2033
2034	if (id > 0 && id <= AH->maxDumpId)
2035		return AH->tocsByDumpId[id];
2036
2037	return NULL;
2038}
2039
2040int
2041TocIDRequired(ArchiveHandle *AH, DumpId id)
2042{
2043	TocEntry   *te = getTocEntryByDumpId(AH, id);
2044
2045	if (!te)
2046		return 0;
2047
2048	return te->reqs;
2049}
2050
2051size_t
2052WriteOffset(ArchiveHandle *AH, pgoff_t o, int wasSet)
2053{
2054	int			off;
2055
2056	/* Save the flag */
2057	AH->WriteBytePtr(AH, wasSet);
2058
2059	/* Write out pgoff_t smallest byte first, prevents endian mismatch */
2060	for (off = 0; off < sizeof(pgoff_t); off++)
2061	{
2062		AH->WriteBytePtr(AH, o & 0xFF);
2063		o >>= 8;
2064	}
2065	return sizeof(pgoff_t) + 1;
2066}
2067
2068int
2069ReadOffset(ArchiveHandle *AH, pgoff_t * o)
2070{
2071	int			i;
2072	int			off;
2073	int			offsetFlg;
2074
2075	/* Initialize to zero */
2076	*o = 0;
2077
2078	/* Check for old version */
2079	if (AH->version < K_VERS_1_7)
2080	{
2081		/* Prior versions wrote offsets using WriteInt */
2082		i = ReadInt(AH);
2083		/* -1 means not set */
2084		if (i < 0)
2085			return K_OFFSET_POS_NOT_SET;
2086		else if (i == 0)
2087			return K_OFFSET_NO_DATA;
2088
2089		/* Cast to pgoff_t because it was written as an int. */
2090		*o = (pgoff_t) i;
2091		return K_OFFSET_POS_SET;
2092	}
2093
2094	/*
2095	 * Read the flag indicating the state of the data pointer. Check if valid
2096	 * and die if not.
2097	 *
2098	 * This used to be handled by a negative or zero pointer, now we use an
2099	 * extra byte specifically for the state.
2100	 */
2101	offsetFlg = AH->ReadBytePtr(AH) & 0xFF;
2102
2103	switch (offsetFlg)
2104	{
2105		case K_OFFSET_POS_NOT_SET:
2106		case K_OFFSET_NO_DATA:
2107		case K_OFFSET_POS_SET:
2108
2109			break;
2110
2111		default:
2112			pg_fatal("unexpected data offset flag %d", offsetFlg);
2113	}
2114
2115	/*
2116	 * Read the bytes
2117	 */
2118	for (off = 0; off < AH->offSize; off++)
2119	{
2120		if (off < sizeof(pgoff_t))
2121			*o |= ((pgoff_t) (AH->ReadBytePtr(AH))) << (off * 8);
2122		else
2123		{
2124			if (AH->ReadBytePtr(AH) != 0)
2125				pg_fatal("file offset in dump file is too large");
2126		}
2127	}
2128
2129	return offsetFlg;
2130}
2131
2132size_t
2133WriteInt(ArchiveHandle *AH, int i)
2134{
2135	int			b;
2136
2137	/*
2138	 * This is a bit yucky, but I don't want to make the binary format very
2139	 * dependent on representation, and not knowing much about it, I write out
2140	 * a sign byte. If you change this, don't forget to change the file
2141	 * version #, and modify ReadInt to read the new format AS WELL AS the old
2142	 * formats.
2143	 */
2144
2145	/* SIGN byte */
2146	if (i < 0)
2147	{
2148		AH->WriteBytePtr(AH, 1);
2149		i = -i;
2150	}
2151	else
2152		AH->WriteBytePtr(AH, 0);
2153
2154	for (b = 0; b < AH->intSize; b++)
2155	{
2156		AH->WriteBytePtr(AH, i & 0xFF);
2157		i >>= 8;
2158	}
2159
2160	return AH->intSize + 1;
2161}
2162
2163int
2164ReadInt(ArchiveHandle *AH)
2165{
2166	int			res = 0;
2167	int			bv,
2168				b;
2169	int			sign = 0;		/* Default positive */
2170	int			bitShift = 0;
2171
2172	if (AH->version > K_VERS_1_0)
2173		/* Read a sign byte */
2174		sign = AH->ReadBytePtr(AH);
2175
2176	for (b = 0; b < AH->intSize; b++)
2177	{
2178		bv = AH->ReadBytePtr(AH) & 0xFF;
2179		if (bv != 0)
2180			res = res + (bv << bitShift);
2181		bitShift += 8;
2182	}
2183
2184	if (sign)
2185		res = -res;
2186
2187	return res;
2188}
2189
2190size_t
2191WriteStr(ArchiveHandle *AH, const char *c)
2192{
2193	size_t		res;
2194
2195	if (c)
2196	{
2197		int			len = strlen(c);
2198
2199		res = WriteInt(AH, len);
2200		AH->WriteBufPtr(AH, c, len);
2201		res += len;
2202	}
2203	else
2204		res = WriteInt(AH, -1);
2205
2206	return res;
2207}
2208
2209char *
2210ReadStr(ArchiveHandle *AH)
2211{
2212	char	   *buf;
2213	int			l;
2214
2215	l = ReadInt(AH);
2216	if (l < 0)
2217		buf = NULL;
2218	else
2219	{
2220		buf = (char *) pg_malloc(l + 1);
2221		AH->ReadBufPtr(AH, buf, l);
2222
2223		buf[l] = '\0';
2224	}
2225
2226	return buf;
2227}
2228
2229static bool
2230_fileExistsInDirectory(const char *dir, const char *filename)
2231{
2232	struct stat st;
2233	char		buf[MAXPGPATH];
2234
2235	if (snprintf(buf, MAXPGPATH, "%s/%s", dir, filename) >= MAXPGPATH)
2236		pg_fatal("directory name too long: \"%s\"", dir);
2237
2238	return (stat(buf, &st) == 0 && S_ISREG(st.st_mode));
2239}
2240
2241static int
2242_discoverArchiveFormat(ArchiveHandle *AH)
2243{
2244	FILE	   *fh;
2245	char		sig[6];			/* More than enough */
2246	size_t		cnt;
2247	int			wantClose = 0;
2248
2249	pg_log_debug("attempting to ascertain archive format");
2250
2251	free(AH->lookahead);
2252
2253	AH->readHeader = 0;
2254	AH->lookaheadSize = 512;
2255	AH->lookahead = pg_malloc0(512);
2256	AH->lookaheadLen = 0;
2257	AH->lookaheadPos = 0;
2258
2259	if (AH->fSpec)
2260	{
2261		struct stat st;
2262
2263		wantClose = 1;
2264
2265		/*
2266		 * Check if the specified archive is a directory. If so, check if
2267		 * there's a "toc.dat" (or "toc.dat.{gz,lz4,zst}") file in it.
2268		 */
2269		if (stat(AH->fSpec, &st) == 0 && S_ISDIR(st.st_mode))
2270		{
2271			AH->format = archDirectory;
2272			if (_fileExistsInDirectory(AH->fSpec, "toc.dat"))
2273				return AH->format;
2274#ifdef HAVE_LIBZ
2275			if (_fileExistsInDirectory(AH->fSpec, "toc.dat.gz"))
2276				return AH->format;
2277#endif
2278#ifdef USE_LZ4
2279			if (_fileExistsInDirectory(AH->fSpec, "toc.dat.lz4"))
2280				return AH->format;
2281#endif
2282#ifdef USE_ZSTD
2283			if (_fileExistsInDirectory(AH->fSpec, "toc.dat.zst"))
2284				return AH->format;
2285#endif
2286			pg_fatal("directory \"%s\" does not appear to be a valid archive (\"toc.dat\" does not exist)",
2287					 AH->fSpec);
2288			fh = NULL;			/* keep compiler quiet */
2289		}
2290		else
2291		{
2292			fh = fopen(AH->fSpec, PG_BINARY_R);
2293			if (!fh)
2294				pg_fatal("could not open input file \"%s\": %m", AH->fSpec);
2295		}
2296	}
2297	else
2298	{
2299		fh = stdin;
2300		if (!fh)
2301			pg_fatal("could not open input file: %m");
2302	}
2303
2304	if ((cnt = fread(sig, 1, 5, fh)) != 5)
2305	{
2306		if (ferror(fh))
2307			pg_fatal("could not read input file: %m");
2308		else
2309			pg_fatal("input file is too short (read %lu, expected 5)",
2310					 (unsigned long) cnt);
2311	}
2312
2313	/* Save it, just in case we need it later */
2314	memcpy(&AH->lookahead[0], sig, 5);
2315	AH->lookaheadLen = 5;
2316
2317	if (strncmp(sig, "PGDMP", 5) == 0)
2318	{
2319		/* It's custom format, stop here */
2320		AH->format = archCustom;
2321		AH->readHeader = 1;
2322	}
2323	else
2324	{
2325		/*
2326		 * *Maybe* we have a tar archive format file or a text dump ... So,
2327		 * read first 512 byte header...
2328		 */
2329		cnt = fread(&AH->lookahead[AH->lookaheadLen], 1, 512 - AH->lookaheadLen, fh);
2330		/* read failure is checked below */
2331		AH->lookaheadLen += cnt;
2332
2333		if (AH->lookaheadLen >= strlen(TEXT_DUMPALL_HEADER) &&
2334			(strncmp(AH->lookahead, TEXT_DUMP_HEADER, strlen(TEXT_DUMP_HEADER)) == 0 ||
2335			 strncmp(AH->lookahead, TEXT_DUMPALL_HEADER, strlen(TEXT_DUMPALL_HEADER)) == 0))
2336		{
2337			/*
2338			 * looks like it's probably a text format dump. so suggest they
2339			 * try psql
2340			 */
2341			pg_fatal("input file appears to be a text format dump. Please use psql.");
2342		}
2343
2344		if (AH->lookaheadLen != 512)
2345		{
2346			if (feof(fh))
2347				pg_fatal("input file does not appear to be a valid archive (too short?)");
2348			else
2349				READ_ERROR_EXIT(fh);
2350		}
2351
2352		if (!isValidTarHeader(AH->lookahead))
2353			pg_fatal("input file does not appear to be a valid tar archive");
2354
2355		AH->format = archTar;
2356	}
2357
2358	/* Close the file if we opened it */
2359	if (wantClose)
2360	{
2361		if (fclose(fh) != 0)
2362			pg_fatal("could not close input file: %m");
2363		/* Forget lookahead, since we'll re-read header after re-opening */
2364		AH->readHeader = 0;
2365		AH->lookaheadLen = 0;
2366	}
2367
2368	return AH->format;
2369}
2370
2371
2372/*
2373 * Allocate an archive handle
2374 */
2375static ArchiveHandle *
2376_allocAH(const char *FileSpec, const ArchiveFormat fmt,
2377		 const pg_compress_specification compression_spec,
2378		 bool dosync, ArchiveMode mode,
2379		 SetupWorkerPtrType setupWorkerPtr, DataDirSyncMethod sync_method)
2380{
2381	ArchiveHandle *AH;
2382	CompressFileHandle *CFH;
2383	pg_compress_specification out_compress_spec = {0};
2384
2385	pg_log_debug("allocating AH for %s, format %d",
2386				 FileSpec ? FileSpec : "(stdio)", fmt);
2387
2388	AH = (ArchiveHandle *) pg_malloc0(sizeof(ArchiveHandle));
2389
2390	AH->version = K_VERS_SELF;
2391
2392	/* initialize for backwards compatible string processing */
2393	AH->public.encoding = 0;	/* PG_SQL_ASCII */
2394	AH->public.std_strings = false;
2395
2396	/* sql error handling */
2397	AH->public.exit_on_error = true;
2398	AH->public.n_errors = 0;
2399
2400	AH->archiveDumpVersion = PG_VERSION;
2401
2402	AH->createDate = time(NULL);
2403
2404	AH->intSize = sizeof(int);
2405	AH->offSize = sizeof(pgoff_t);
2406	if (FileSpec)
2407	{
2408		AH->fSpec = pg_strdup(FileSpec);
2409
2410		/*
2411		 * Not used; maybe later....
2412		 *
2413		 * AH->workDir = pg_strdup(FileSpec); for(i=strlen(FileSpec) ; i > 0 ;
2414		 * i--) if (AH->workDir[i-1] == '/')
2415		 */
2416	}
2417	else
2418		AH->fSpec = NULL;
2419
2420	AH->currUser = NULL;		/* unknown */
2421	AH->currSchema = NULL;		/* ditto */
2422	AH->currTablespace = NULL;	/* ditto */
2423	AH->currTableAm = NULL;		/* ditto */
2424
2425	AH->toc = (TocEntry *) pg_malloc0(sizeof(TocEntry));
2426
2427	AH->toc->next = AH->toc;
2428	AH->toc->prev = AH->toc;
2429
2430	AH->mode = mode;
2431	AH->compression_spec = compression_spec;
2432	AH->dosync = dosync;
2433	AH->sync_method = sync_method;
2434
2435	memset(&(AH->sqlparse), 0, sizeof(AH->sqlparse));
2436
2437	/* Open stdout with no compression for AH output handle */
2438	out_compress_spec.algorithm = PG_COMPRESSION_NONE;
2439	CFH = InitCompressFileHandle(out_compress_spec);
2440	if (!CFH->open_func(NULL, fileno(stdout), PG_BINARY_A, CFH))
2441		pg_fatal("could not open stdout for appending: %m");
2442	AH->OF = CFH;
2443
2444	/*
2445	 * On Windows, we need to use binary mode to read/write non-text files,
2446	 * which include all archive formats as well as compressed plain text.
2447	 * Force stdin/stdout into binary mode if that is what we are using.
2448	 */
2449#ifdef WIN32
2450	if ((fmt != archNull || compression_spec.algorithm != PG_COMPRESSION_NONE) &&
2451		(AH->fSpec == NULL || strcmp(AH->fSpec, "") == 0))
2452	{
2453		if (mode == archModeWrite)
2454			_setmode(fileno(stdout), O_BINARY);
2455		else
2456			_setmode(fileno(stdin), O_BINARY);
2457	}
2458#endif
2459
2460	AH->SetupWorkerPtr = setupWorkerPtr;
2461
2462	if (fmt == archUnknown)
2463		AH->format = _discoverArchiveFormat(AH);
2464	else
2465		AH->format = fmt;
2466
2467	switch (AH->format)
2468	{
2469		case archCustom:
2470			InitArchiveFmt_Custom(AH);
2471			break;
2472
2473		case archNull:
2474			InitArchiveFmt_Null(AH);
2475			break;
2476
2477		case archDirectory:
2478			InitArchiveFmt_Directory(AH);
2479			break;
2480
2481		case archTar:
2482			InitArchiveFmt_Tar(AH);
2483			break;
2484
2485		default:
2486			pg_fatal("unrecognized file format \"%d\"", AH->format);
2487	}
2488
2489	return AH;
2490}
2491
2492/*
2493 * Write out all data (tables & LOs)
2494 */
2495void
2496WriteDataChunks(ArchiveHandle *AH, ParallelState *pstate)
2497{
2498	TocEntry   *te;
2499
2500	if (pstate && pstate->numWorkers > 1)
2501	{
2502		/*
2503		 * In parallel mode, this code runs in the leader process.  We
2504		 * construct an array of candidate TEs, then sort it into decreasing
2505		 * size order, then dispatch each TE to a data-transfer worker.  By
2506		 * dumping larger tables first, we avoid getting into a situation
2507		 * where we're down to one job and it's big, losing parallelism.
2508		 */
2509		TocEntry  **tes;
2510		int			ntes;
2511
2512		tes = (TocEntry **) pg_malloc(AH->tocCount * sizeof(TocEntry *));
2513		ntes = 0;
2514		for (te = AH->toc->next; te != AH->toc; te = te->next)
2515		{
2516			/* Consider only TEs with dataDumper functions ... */
2517			if (!te->dataDumper)
2518				continue;
2519			/* ... and ignore ones not enabled for dump */
2520			if ((te->reqs & REQ_DATA) == 0)
2521				continue;
2522
2523			tes[ntes++] = te;
2524		}
2525
2526		if (ntes > 1)
2527			qsort(tes, ntes, sizeof(TocEntry *), TocEntrySizeCompareQsort);
2528
2529		for (int i = 0; i < ntes; i++)
2530			DispatchJobForTocEntry(AH, pstate, tes[i], ACT_DUMP,
2531								   mark_dump_job_done, NULL);
2532
2533		pg_free(tes);
2534
2535		/* Now wait for workers to finish. */
2536		WaitForWorkers(AH, pstate, WFW_ALL_IDLE);
2537	}
2538	else
2539	{
2540		/* Non-parallel mode: just dump all candidate TEs sequentially. */
2541		for (te = AH->toc->next; te != AH->toc; te = te->next)
2542		{
2543			/* Must have same filter conditions as above */
2544			if (!te->dataDumper)
2545				continue;
2546			if ((te->reqs & REQ_DATA) == 0)
2547				continue;
2548
2549			WriteDataChunksForTocEntry(AH, te);
2550		}
2551	}
2552}
2553
2554
2555/*
2556 * Callback function that's invoked in the leader process after a step has
2557 * been parallel dumped.
2558 *
2559 * We don't need to do anything except check for worker failure.
2560 */
2561static void
2562mark_dump_job_done(ArchiveHandle *AH,
2563				   TocEntry *te,
2564				   int status,
2565				   void *callback_data)
2566{
2567	pg_log_info("finished item %d %s %s",
2568				te->dumpId, te->desc, te->tag);
2569
2570	if (status != 0)
2571		pg_fatal("worker process failed: exit code %d",
2572				 status);
2573}
2574
2575
2576void
2577WriteDataChunksForTocEntry(ArchiveHandle *AH, TocEntry *te)
2578{
2579	StartDataPtrType startPtr;
2580	EndDataPtrType endPtr;
2581
2582	AH->currToc = te;
2583
2584	if (strcmp(te->desc, "BLOBS") == 0)
2585	{
2586		startPtr = AH->StartLOsPtr;
2587		endPtr = AH->EndLOsPtr;
2588	}
2589	else
2590	{
2591		startPtr = AH->StartDataPtr;
2592		endPtr = AH->EndDataPtr;
2593	}
2594
2595	if (startPtr != NULL)
2596		(*startPtr) (AH, te);
2597
2598	/*
2599	 * The user-provided DataDumper routine needs to call AH->WriteData
2600	 */
2601	te->dataDumper((Archive *) AH, te->dataDumperArg);
2602
2603	if (endPtr != NULL)
2604		(*endPtr) (AH, te);
2605
2606	AH->currToc = NULL;
2607}
2608
2609void
2610WriteToc(ArchiveHandle *AH)
2611{
2612	TocEntry   *te;
2613	char		workbuf[32];
2614	int			tocCount;
2615	int			i;
2616
2617	/* count entries that will actually be dumped */
2618	tocCount = 0;
2619	for (te = AH->toc->next; te != AH->toc; te = te->next)
2620	{
2621		if ((te->reqs & (REQ_SCHEMA | REQ_DATA | REQ_STATS | REQ_SPECIAL)) != 0)
2622			tocCount++;
2623	}
2624
2625	/* printf("%d TOC Entries to save\n", tocCount); */
2626
2627	WriteInt(AH, tocCount);
2628
2629	for (te = AH->toc->next; te != AH->toc; te = te->next)
2630	{
2631		if ((te->reqs & (REQ_SCHEMA | REQ_DATA | REQ_STATS | REQ_SPECIAL)) == 0)
2632			continue;
2633
2634		WriteInt(AH, te->dumpId);
2635		WriteInt(AH, te->dataDumper ? 1 : 0);
2636
2637		/* OID is recorded as a string for historical reasons */
2638		sprintf(workbuf, "%u", te->catalogId.tableoid);
2639		WriteStr(AH, workbuf);
2640		sprintf(workbuf, "%u", te->catalogId.oid);
2641		WriteStr(AH, workbuf);
2642
2643		WriteStr(AH, te->tag);
2644		WriteStr(AH, te->desc);
2645		WriteInt(AH, te->section);
2646
2647		if (te->defnLen)
2648		{
2649			/*
2650			 * defnLen should only be set for custom format's second call to
2651			 * WriteToc(), which rewrites the TOC in place to update data
2652			 * offsets.  Instead of calling the defnDumper a second time
2653			 * (which could involve re-executing queries), just skip writing
2654			 * the entry.  While regenerating the definition should
2655			 * theoretically produce the same result as before, it's expensive
2656			 * and feels risky.
2657			 *
2658			 * The custom format only calls WriteToc() a second time if
2659			 * fseeko() is usable (see _CloseArchive() in pg_backup_custom.c),
2660			 * so we can safely use it without checking.  For other formats,
2661			 * we fail because one of our assumptions must no longer hold
2662			 * true.
2663			 *
2664			 * XXX This is a layering violation, but the alternative is an
2665			 * awkward and complicated callback infrastructure for this
2666			 * special case.  This might be worth revisiting in the future.
2667			 */
2668			if (AH->format != archCustom)
2669				pg_fatal("unexpected TOC entry in WriteToc(): %d %s %s",
2670						 te->dumpId, te->desc, te->tag);
2671
2672			if (fseeko(AH->FH, te->defnLen, SEEK_CUR) != 0)
2673				pg_fatal("error during file seek: %m");
2674		}
2675		else if (te->defnDumper)
2676		{
2677			char	   *defn = te->defnDumper((Archive *) AH, te->defnDumperArg, te);
2678
2679			te->defnLen = WriteStr(AH, defn);
2680			pg_free(defn);
2681		}
2682		else
2683			WriteStr(AH, te->defn);
2684
2685		WriteStr(AH, te->dropStmt);
2686		WriteStr(AH, te->copyStmt);
2687		WriteStr(AH, te->namespace);
2688		WriteStr(AH, te->tablespace);
2689		WriteStr(AH, te->tableam);
2690		WriteInt(AH, te->relkind);
2691		WriteStr(AH, te->owner);
2692		WriteStr(AH, "false");
2693
2694		/* Dump list of dependencies */
2695		for (i = 0; i < te->nDeps; i++)
2696		{
2697			sprintf(workbuf, "%d", te->dependencies[i]);
2698			WriteStr(AH, workbuf);
2699		}
2700		WriteStr(AH, NULL);		/* Terminate List */
2701
2702		if (AH->WriteExtraTocPtr)
2703			AH->WriteExtraTocPtr(AH, te);
2704	}
2705}
2706
2707void
2708ReadToc(ArchiveHandle *AH)
2709{
2710	int			i;
2711	char	   *tmp;
2712	DumpId	   *deps;
2713	int			depIdx;
2714	int			depSize;
2715	TocEntry   *te;
2716	bool		is_supported;
2717
2718	AH->tocCount = ReadInt(AH);
2719	AH->maxDumpId = 0;
2720
2721	for (i = 0; i < AH->tocCount; i++)
2722	{
2723		te = (TocEntry *) pg_malloc0(sizeof(TocEntry));
2724		te->dumpId = ReadInt(AH);
2725
2726		if (te->dumpId > AH->maxDumpId)
2727			AH->maxDumpId = te->dumpId;
2728
2729		/* Sanity check */
2730		if (te->dumpId <= 0)
2731			pg_fatal("entry ID %d out of range -- perhaps a corrupt TOC",
2732					 te->dumpId);
2733
2734		te->hadDumper = ReadInt(AH);
2735
2736		if (AH->version >= K_VERS_1_8)
2737		{
2738			tmp = ReadStr(AH);
2739			sscanf(tmp, "%u", &te->catalogId.tableoid);
2740			free(tmp);
2741		}
2742		else
2743			te->catalogId.tableoid = InvalidOid;
2744		tmp = ReadStr(AH);
2745		sscanf(tmp, "%u", &te->catalogId.oid);
2746		free(tmp);
2747
2748		te->tag = ReadStr(AH);
2749		te->desc = ReadStr(AH);
2750
2751		if (AH->version >= K_VERS_1_11)
2752		{
2753			te->section = ReadInt(AH);
2754		}
2755		else
2756		{
2757			/*
2758			 * Rules for pre-8.4 archives wherein pg_dump hasn't classified
2759			 * the entries into sections.  This list need not cover entry
2760			 * types added later than 8.4.
2761			 */
2762			if (strcmp(te->desc, "COMMENT") == 0 ||
2763				strcmp(te->desc, "ACL") == 0 ||
2764				strcmp(te->desc, "ACL LANGUAGE") == 0)
2765				te->section = SECTION_NONE;
2766			else if (strcmp(te->desc, "TABLE DATA") == 0 ||
