2767					 strcmp(te->desc, "BLOBS") == 0 ||
2768					 strcmp(te->desc, "BLOB COMMENTS") == 0)
2769				te->section = SECTION_DATA;
2770			else if (strcmp(te->desc, "CONSTRAINT") == 0 ||
2771					 strcmp(te->desc, "CHECK CONSTRAINT") == 0 ||
2772					 strcmp(te->desc, "FK CONSTRAINT") == 0 ||
2773					 strcmp(te->desc, "INDEX") == 0 ||
2774					 strcmp(te->desc, "RULE") == 0 ||
2775					 strcmp(te->desc, "TRIGGER") == 0)
2776				te->section = SECTION_POST_DATA;
2777			else
2778				te->section = SECTION_PRE_DATA;
2779		}
2780
2781		te->defn = ReadStr(AH);
2782		te->dropStmt = ReadStr(AH);
2783
2784		if (AH->version >= K_VERS_1_3)
2785			te->copyStmt = ReadStr(AH);
2786
2787		if (AH->version >= K_VERS_1_6)
2788			te->namespace = ReadStr(AH);
2789
2790		if (AH->version >= K_VERS_1_10)
2791			te->tablespace = ReadStr(AH);
2792
2793		if (AH->version >= K_VERS_1_14)
2794			te->tableam = ReadStr(AH);
2795
2796		if (AH->version >= K_VERS_1_16)
2797			te->relkind = ReadInt(AH);
2798
2799		te->owner = ReadStr(AH);
2800		is_supported = true;
2801		if (AH->version < K_VERS_1_9)
2802			is_supported = false;
2803		else
2804		{
2805			tmp = ReadStr(AH);
2806
2807			if (strcmp(tmp, "true") == 0)
2808				is_supported = false;
2809
2810			free(tmp);
2811		}
2812
2813		if (!is_supported)
2814			pg_log_warning("restoring tables WITH OIDS is not supported anymore");
2815
2816		/* Read TOC entry dependencies */
2817		if (AH->version >= K_VERS_1_5)
2818		{
2819			depSize = 100;
2820			deps = (DumpId *) pg_malloc(sizeof(DumpId) * depSize);
2821			depIdx = 0;
2822			for (;;)
2823			{
2824				tmp = ReadStr(AH);
2825				if (!tmp)
2826					break;		/* end of list */
2827				if (depIdx >= depSize)
2828				{
2829					depSize *= 2;
2830					deps = (DumpId *) pg_realloc(deps, sizeof(DumpId) * depSize);
2831				}
2832				sscanf(tmp, "%d", &deps[depIdx]);
2833				free(tmp);
2834				depIdx++;
2835			}
2836
2837			if (depIdx > 0)		/* We have a non-null entry */
2838			{
2839				deps = (DumpId *) pg_realloc(deps, sizeof(DumpId) * depIdx);
2840				te->dependencies = deps;
2841				te->nDeps = depIdx;
2842			}
2843			else
2844			{
2845				free(deps);
2846				te->dependencies = NULL;
2847				te->nDeps = 0;
2848			}
2849		}
2850		else
2851		{
2852			te->dependencies = NULL;
2853			te->nDeps = 0;
2854		}
2855		te->dataLength = 0;
2856
2857		if (AH->ReadExtraTocPtr)
2858			AH->ReadExtraTocPtr(AH, te);
2859
2860		pg_log_debug("read TOC entry %d (ID %d) for %s %s",
2861					 i, te->dumpId, te->desc, te->tag);
2862
2863		/* link completed entry into TOC circular list */
2864		te->prev = AH->toc->prev;
2865		AH->toc->prev->next = te;
2866		AH->toc->prev = te;
2867		te->next = AH->toc;
2868
2869		/* special processing immediately upon read for some items */
2870		if (strcmp(te->desc, "ENCODING") == 0)
2871			processEncodingEntry(AH, te);
2872		else if (strcmp(te->desc, "STDSTRINGS") == 0)
2873			processStdStringsEntry(AH, te);
2874		else if (strcmp(te->desc, "SEARCHPATH") == 0)
2875			processSearchPathEntry(AH, te);
2876	}
2877}
2878
2879static void
2880processEncodingEntry(ArchiveHandle *AH, TocEntry *te)
2881{
2882	/* te->defn should have the form SET client_encoding = 'foo'; */
2883	char	   *defn = pg_strdup(te->defn);
2884	char	   *ptr1;
2885	char	   *ptr2 = NULL;
2886	int			encoding;
2887
2888	ptr1 = strchr(defn, '\'');
2889	if (ptr1)
2890		ptr2 = strchr(++ptr1, '\'');
2891	if (ptr2)
2892	{
2893		*ptr2 = '\0';
2894		encoding = pg_char_to_encoding(ptr1);
2895		if (encoding < 0)
2896			pg_fatal("unrecognized encoding \"%s\"",
2897					 ptr1);
2898		AH->public.encoding = encoding;
2899		setFmtEncoding(encoding);
2900	}
2901	else
2902		pg_fatal("invalid ENCODING item: %s",
2903				 te->defn);
2904
2905	free(defn);
2906}
2907
2908static void
2909processStdStringsEntry(ArchiveHandle *AH, TocEntry *te)
2910{
2911	/* te->defn should have the form SET standard_conforming_strings = 'x'; */
2912	char	   *ptr1;
2913
2914	ptr1 = strchr(te->defn, '\'');
2915	if (ptr1 && strncmp(ptr1, "'on'", 4) == 0)
2916		AH->public.std_strings = true;
2917	else if (ptr1 && strncmp(ptr1, "'off'", 5) == 0)
2918		AH->public.std_strings = false;
2919	else
2920		pg_fatal("invalid STDSTRINGS item: %s",
2921				 te->defn);
2922}
2923
2924static void
2925processSearchPathEntry(ArchiveHandle *AH, TocEntry *te)
2926{
2927	/*
2928	 * te->defn should contain a command to set search_path.  We just copy it
2929	 * verbatim for use later.
2930	 */
2931	AH->public.searchpath = pg_strdup(te->defn);
2932}
2933
2934static void
2935StrictNamesCheck(RestoreOptions *ropt)
2936{
2937	const char *missing_name;
2938
2939	Assert(ropt->strict_names);
2940
2941	if (ropt->schemaNames.head != NULL)
2942	{
2943		missing_name = simple_string_list_not_touched(&ropt->schemaNames);
2944		if (missing_name != NULL)
2945			pg_fatal("schema \"%s\" not found", missing_name);
2946	}
2947
2948	if (ropt->tableNames.head != NULL)
2949	{
2950		missing_name = simple_string_list_not_touched(&ropt->tableNames);
2951		if (missing_name != NULL)
2952			pg_fatal("table \"%s\" not found", missing_name);
2953	}
2954
2955	if (ropt->indexNames.head != NULL)
2956	{
2957		missing_name = simple_string_list_not_touched(&ropt->indexNames);
2958		if (missing_name != NULL)
2959			pg_fatal("index \"%s\" not found", missing_name);
2960	}
2961
2962	if (ropt->functionNames.head != NULL)
2963	{
2964		missing_name = simple_string_list_not_touched(&ropt->functionNames);
2965		if (missing_name != NULL)
2966			pg_fatal("function \"%s\" not found", missing_name);
2967	}
2968
2969	if (ropt->triggerNames.head != NULL)
2970	{
2971		missing_name = simple_string_list_not_touched(&ropt->triggerNames);
2972		if (missing_name != NULL)
2973			pg_fatal("trigger \"%s\" not found", missing_name);
2974	}
2975}
2976
2977/*
2978 * Determine whether we want to restore this TOC entry.
2979 *
2980 * Returns 0 if entry should be skipped, or some combination of the
2981 * REQ_SCHEMA, REQ_DATA, and REQ_STATS bits if we want to restore schema, data
2982 * and/or statistics portions of this TOC entry, or REQ_SPECIAL if it's a
2983 * special entry.
2984 */
2985static int
2986_tocEntryRequired(TocEntry *te, teSection curSection, ArchiveHandle *AH)
2987{
2988	int			res = REQ_SCHEMA | REQ_DATA;
2989	RestoreOptions *ropt = AH->public.ropt;
2990
2991	/* These items are treated specially */
2992	if (strcmp(te->desc, "ENCODING") == 0 ||
2993		strcmp(te->desc, "STDSTRINGS") == 0 ||
2994		strcmp(te->desc, "SEARCHPATH") == 0)
2995		return REQ_SPECIAL;
2996
2997	if (strcmp(te->desc, "STATISTICS DATA") == 0)
2998	{
2999		if (!ropt->dumpStatistics)
3000			return 0;
3001
3002		res = REQ_STATS;
3003	}
3004
3005	/*
3006	 * DATABASE and DATABASE PROPERTIES also have a special rule: they are
3007	 * restored in createDB mode, and not restored otherwise, independently of
3008	 * all else.
3009	 */
3010	if (strcmp(te->desc, "DATABASE") == 0 ||
3011		strcmp(te->desc, "DATABASE PROPERTIES") == 0)
3012	{
3013		if (ropt->createDB)
3014			return REQ_SCHEMA;
3015		else
3016			return 0;
3017	}
3018
3019	/*
3020	 * Process exclusions that affect certain classes of TOC entries.
3021	 */
3022
3023	/* If it's an ACL, maybe ignore it */
3024	if (ropt->aclsSkip && _tocEntryIsACL(te))
3025		return 0;
3026
3027	/* If it's a comment, maybe ignore it */
3028	if (ropt->no_comments && strcmp(te->desc, "COMMENT") == 0)
3029		return 0;
3030
3031	/* If it's a policy, maybe ignore it */
3032	if (ropt->no_policies &&
3033		(strcmp(te->desc, "POLICY") == 0 ||
3034		 strcmp(te->desc, "ROW SECURITY") == 0))
3035		return 0;
3036
3037	/*
3038	 * If it's a comment on a policy, a publication, or a subscription, maybe
3039	 * ignore it.
3040	 */
3041	if (strcmp(te->desc, "COMMENT") == 0)
3042	{
3043		if (ropt->no_policies &&
3044			strncmp(te->tag, "POLICY", strlen("POLICY")) == 0)
3045			return 0;
3046
3047		if (ropt->no_publications &&
3048			strncmp(te->tag, "PUBLICATION", strlen("PUBLICATION")) == 0)
3049			return 0;
3050
3051		if (ropt->no_subscriptions &&
3052			strncmp(te->tag, "SUBSCRIPTION", strlen("SUBSCRIPTION")) == 0)
3053			return 0;
3054	}
3055
3056	/*
3057	 * If it's a publication or a table part of a publication, maybe ignore
3058	 * it.
3059	 */
3060	if (ropt->no_publications &&
3061		(strcmp(te->desc, "PUBLICATION") == 0 ||
3062		 strcmp(te->desc, "PUBLICATION TABLE") == 0 ||
3063		 strcmp(te->desc, "PUBLICATION TABLES IN SCHEMA") == 0))
3064		return 0;
3065
3066	/* If it's a security label, maybe ignore it */
3067	if (ropt->no_security_labels && strcmp(te->desc, "SECURITY LABEL") == 0)
3068		return 0;
3069
3070	/*
3071	 * If it's a security label on a publication or a subscription, maybe
3072	 * ignore it.
3073	 */
3074	if (strcmp(te->desc, "SECURITY LABEL") == 0)
3075	{
3076		if (ropt->no_publications &&
3077			strncmp(te->tag, "PUBLICATION", strlen("PUBLICATION")) == 0)
3078			return 0;
3079
3080		if (ropt->no_subscriptions &&
3081			strncmp(te->tag, "SUBSCRIPTION", strlen("SUBSCRIPTION")) == 0)
3082			return 0;
3083	}
3084
3085	/* If it's a subscription, maybe ignore it */
3086	if (ropt->no_subscriptions && strcmp(te->desc, "SUBSCRIPTION") == 0)
3087		return 0;
3088
3089	/* Ignore it if section is not to be dumped/restored */
3090	switch (curSection)
3091	{
3092		case SECTION_PRE_DATA:
3093			if (!(ropt->dumpSections & DUMP_PRE_DATA))
3094				return 0;
3095			break;
3096		case SECTION_DATA:
3097			if (!(ropt->dumpSections & DUMP_DATA))
3098				return 0;
3099			break;
3100		case SECTION_POST_DATA:
3101			if (!(ropt->dumpSections & DUMP_POST_DATA))
3102				return 0;
3103			break;
3104		default:
3105			/* shouldn't get here, really, but ignore it */
3106			return 0;
3107	}
3108
3109	/* Ignore it if rejected by idWanted[] (cf. SortTocFromFile) */
3110	if (ropt->idWanted && !ropt->idWanted[te->dumpId - 1])
3111		return 0;
3112
3113	/*
3114	 * Check options for selective dump/restore.
3115	 */
3116	if (strcmp(te->desc, "ACL") == 0 ||
3117		strcmp(te->desc, "COMMENT") == 0 ||
3118		strcmp(te->desc, "STATISTICS DATA") == 0 ||
3119		strcmp(te->desc, "SECURITY LABEL") == 0)
3120	{
3121		/* Database properties react to createDB, not selectivity options. */
3122		if (strncmp(te->tag, "DATABASE ", 9) == 0)
3123		{
3124			if (!ropt->createDB)
3125				return 0;
3126		}
3127		else if (ropt->schemaNames.head != NULL ||
3128				 ropt->schemaExcludeNames.head != NULL ||
3129				 ropt->selTypes)
3130		{
3131			/*
3132			 * In a selective dump/restore, we want to restore these dependent
3133			 * TOC entry types only if their parent object is being restored.
3134			 * Without selectivity options, we let through everything in the
3135			 * archive.  Note there may be such entries with no parent, eg
3136			 * non-default ACLs for built-in objects.  Also, we make
3137			 * per-column ACLs additionally depend on the table's ACL if any
3138			 * to ensure correct restore order, so those dependencies should
3139			 * be ignored in this check.
3140			 *
3141			 * This code depends on the parent having been marked already,
3142			 * which should be the case; if it isn't, perhaps due to
3143			 * SortTocFromFile rearrangement, skipping the dependent entry
3144			 * seems prudent anyway.
3145			 *
3146			 * Ideally we'd handle, eg, table CHECK constraints this way too.
3147			 * But it's hard to tell which of their dependencies is the one to
3148			 * consult.
3149			 */
3150			bool		dumpthis = false;
3151
3152			for (int i = 0; i < te->nDeps; i++)
3153			{
3154				TocEntry   *pte = getTocEntryByDumpId(AH, te->dependencies[i]);
3155
3156				if (!pte)
3157					continue;	/* probably shouldn't happen */
3158				if (strcmp(pte->desc, "ACL") == 0)
3159					continue;	/* ignore dependency on another ACL */
3160				if (pte->reqs == 0)
3161					continue;	/* this object isn't marked, so ignore it */
3162				/* Found a parent to be dumped, so we want to dump this too */
3163				dumpthis = true;
3164				break;
3165			}
3166			if (!dumpthis)
3167				return 0;
3168		}
3169	}
3170	else
3171	{
3172		/* Apply selective-restore rules for standalone TOC entries. */
3173		if (ropt->schemaNames.head != NULL)
3174		{
3175			/* If no namespace is specified, it means all. */
3176			if (!te->namespace)
3177				return 0;
3178			if (!simple_string_list_member(&ropt->schemaNames, te->namespace))
3179				return 0;
3180		}
3181
3182		if (ropt->schemaExcludeNames.head != NULL &&
3183			te->namespace &&
3184			simple_string_list_member(&ropt->schemaExcludeNames, te->namespace))
3185			return 0;
3186
3187		if (ropt->selTypes)
3188		{
3189			if (strcmp(te->desc, "TABLE") == 0 ||
3190				strcmp(te->desc, "TABLE DATA") == 0 ||
3191				strcmp(te->desc, "VIEW") == 0 ||
3192				strcmp(te->desc, "FOREIGN TABLE") == 0 ||
3193				strcmp(te->desc, "MATERIALIZED VIEW") == 0 ||
3194				strcmp(te->desc, "MATERIALIZED VIEW DATA") == 0 ||
3195				strcmp(te->desc, "SEQUENCE") == 0 ||
3196				strcmp(te->desc, "SEQUENCE SET") == 0)
3197			{
3198				if (!ropt->selTable)
3199					return 0;
3200				if (ropt->tableNames.head != NULL &&
3201					!simple_string_list_member(&ropt->tableNames, te->tag))
3202					return 0;
3203			}
3204			else if (strcmp(te->desc, "INDEX") == 0)
3205			{
3206				if (!ropt->selIndex)
3207					return 0;
3208				if (ropt->indexNames.head != NULL &&
3209					!simple_string_list_member(&ropt->indexNames, te->tag))
3210					return 0;
3211			}
3212			else if (strcmp(te->desc, "FUNCTION") == 0 ||
3213					 strcmp(te->desc, "AGGREGATE") == 0 ||
3214					 strcmp(te->desc, "PROCEDURE") == 0)
3215			{
3216				if (!ropt->selFunction)
3217					return 0;
3218				if (ropt->functionNames.head != NULL &&
3219					!simple_string_list_member(&ropt->functionNames, te->tag))
3220					return 0;
3221			}
3222			else if (strcmp(te->desc, "TRIGGER") == 0)
3223			{
3224				if (!ropt->selTrigger)
3225					return 0;
3226				if (ropt->triggerNames.head != NULL &&
3227					!simple_string_list_member(&ropt->triggerNames, te->tag))
3228					return 0;
3229			}
3230			else
3231				return 0;
3232		}
3233	}
3234
3235
3236	/*
3237	 * Determine whether the TOC entry contains schema and/or data components,
3238	 * and mask off inapplicable REQ bits.  If it had a dataDumper, assume
3239	 * it's both schema and data.  Otherwise it's probably schema-only, but
3240	 * there are exceptions.
3241	 */
3242	if (!te->hadDumper)
3243	{
3244		/*
3245		 * Special Case: If 'SEQUENCE SET' or anything to do with LOs, then it
3246		 * is considered a data entry.  We don't need to check for BLOBS or
3247		 * old-style BLOB COMMENTS entries, because they will have hadDumper =
3248		 * true ... but we do need to check new-style BLOB ACLs, comments,
3249		 * etc.
3250		 */
3251		if (strcmp(te->desc, "SEQUENCE SET") == 0 ||
3252			strcmp(te->desc, "BLOB") == 0 ||
3253			strcmp(te->desc, "BLOB METADATA") == 0 ||
3254			(strcmp(te->desc, "ACL") == 0 &&
3255			 strncmp(te->tag, "LARGE OBJECT", 12) == 0) ||
3256			(strcmp(te->desc, "COMMENT") == 0 &&
3257			 strncmp(te->tag, "LARGE OBJECT", 12) == 0) ||
3258			(strcmp(te->desc, "SECURITY LABEL") == 0 &&
3259			 strncmp(te->tag, "LARGE OBJECT", 12) == 0))
3260			res = res & REQ_DATA;
3261		else
3262			res = res & ~REQ_DATA;
3263	}
3264
3265	/*
3266	 * If there's no definition command, there's no schema component.  Treat
3267	 * "load via partition root" comments as not schema.
3268	 */
3269	if (!te->defn || !te->defn[0] ||
3270		strncmp(te->defn, "-- load via partition root ", 27) == 0)
3271		res = res & ~REQ_SCHEMA;
3272
3273	/*
3274	 * Special case: <Init> type with <Max OID> tag; this is obsolete and we
3275	 * always ignore it.
3276	 */
3277	if ((strcmp(te->desc, "<Init>") == 0) && (strcmp(te->tag, "Max OID") == 0))
3278		return 0;
3279
3280	/* Mask it if we don't want data */
3281	if (!ropt->dumpData)
3282	{
3283		/*
3284		 * The sequence_data option overrides dumpData for SEQUENCE SET.
3285		 *
3286		 * In binary-upgrade mode, even with dumpData unset, we do not mask
3287		 * out large objects.  (Only large object definitions, comments and
3288		 * other metadata should be generated in binary-upgrade mode, not the
3289		 * actual data, but that need not concern us here.)
3290		 */
3291		if (!(ropt->sequence_data && strcmp(te->desc, "SEQUENCE SET") == 0) &&
3292			!(ropt->binary_upgrade &&
3293			  (strcmp(te->desc, "BLOB") == 0 ||
3294			   strcmp(te->desc, "BLOB METADATA") == 0 ||
3295			   (strcmp(te->desc, "ACL") == 0 &&
3296				strncmp(te->tag, "LARGE OBJECT", 12) == 0) ||
3297			   (strcmp(te->desc, "COMMENT") == 0 &&
3298				strncmp(te->tag, "LARGE OBJECT", 12) == 0) ||
3299			   (strcmp(te->desc, "SECURITY LABEL") == 0 &&
3300				strncmp(te->tag, "LARGE OBJECT", 12) == 0))))
3301			res = res & (REQ_SCHEMA | REQ_STATS);
3302	}
3303
3304	/* Mask it if we don't want schema */
3305	if (!ropt->dumpSchema)
3306		res = res & (REQ_DATA | REQ_STATS);
3307
3308	return res;
3309}
3310
3311/*
3312 * Identify which pass we should restore this TOC entry in.
3313 *
3314 * See notes with the RestorePass typedef in pg_backup_archiver.h.
3315 */
3316static RestorePass
3317_tocEntryRestorePass(TocEntry *te)
3318{
3319	/* "ACL LANGUAGE" was a crock emitted only in PG 7.4 */
3320	if (strcmp(te->desc, "ACL") == 0 ||
3321		strcmp(te->desc, "ACL LANGUAGE") == 0 ||
3322		strcmp(te->desc, "DEFAULT ACL") == 0)
3323		return RESTORE_PASS_ACL;
3324	if (strcmp(te->desc, "EVENT TRIGGER") == 0 ||
3325		strcmp(te->desc, "MATERIALIZED VIEW DATA") == 0)
3326		return RESTORE_PASS_POST_ACL;
3327
3328	/*
3329	 * Comments and security labels need to be emitted in the same pass as
3330	 * their parent objects. ACLs haven't got comments and security labels,
3331	 * and neither do matview data objects, but event triggers do.
3332	 * (Fortunately, event triggers haven't got ACLs, or we'd need yet another
3333	 * weird special case.)
3334	 */
3335	if ((strcmp(te->desc, "COMMENT") == 0 ||
3336		 strcmp(te->desc, "SECURITY LABEL") == 0) &&
3337		strncmp(te->tag, "EVENT TRIGGER ", 14) == 0)
3338		return RESTORE_PASS_POST_ACL;
3339
3340	/*
3341	 * If statistics data is dependent on materialized view data, it must be
3342	 * deferred to RESTORE_PASS_POST_ACL.  Those entries are already marked as
3343	 * SECTION_POST_DATA, and some other stats entries (e.g., index stats)
3344	 * will also be marked as SECTION_POST_DATA.  Additionally, our lookahead
3345	 * code in fetchAttributeStats() assumes that we dump all statistics data
3346	 * entries in TOC order.  To ensure this assumption holds, we move all
3347	 * statistics data entries in SECTION_POST_DATA to RESTORE_PASS_POST_ACL.
3348	 */
3349	if (strcmp(te->desc, "STATISTICS DATA") == 0 &&
3350		te->section == SECTION_POST_DATA)
3351		return RESTORE_PASS_POST_ACL;
3352
3353	/* All else can be handled in the main pass. */
3354	return RESTORE_PASS_MAIN;
3355}
3356
3357/*
3358 * Identify TOC entries that are ACLs.
3359 *
3360 * Note: it seems worth duplicating some code here to avoid a hard-wired
3361 * assumption that these are exactly the same entries that we restore during
3362 * the RESTORE_PASS_ACL phase.
3363 */
3364static bool
3365_tocEntryIsACL(TocEntry *te)
3366{
3367	/* "ACL LANGUAGE" was a crock emitted only in PG 7.4 */
3368	if (strcmp(te->desc, "ACL") == 0 ||
3369		strcmp(te->desc, "ACL LANGUAGE") == 0 ||
3370		strcmp(te->desc, "DEFAULT ACL") == 0)
3371		return true;
3372	return false;
3373}
3374
3375/*
3376 * Issue SET commands for parameters that we want to have set the same way
3377 * at all times during execution of a restore script.
3378 */
3379static void
3380_doSetFixedOutputState(ArchiveHandle *AH)
3381{
3382	RestoreOptions *ropt = AH->public.ropt;
3383
3384	/*
3385	 * Disable timeouts to allow for slow commands, idle parallel workers, etc
3386	 */
3387	ahprintf(AH, "SET statement_timeout = 0;\n");
3388	ahprintf(AH, "SET lock_timeout = 0;\n");
3389	ahprintf(AH, "SET idle_in_transaction_session_timeout = 0;\n");
3390	ahprintf(AH, "SET transaction_timeout = 0;\n");
3391
3392	/* Select the correct character set encoding */
3393	ahprintf(AH, "SET client_encoding = '%s';\n",
3394			 pg_encoding_to_char(AH->public.encoding));
3395
3396	/* Select the correct string literal syntax */
3397	ahprintf(AH, "SET standard_conforming_strings = %s;\n",
3398			 AH->public.std_strings ? "on" : "off");
3399
3400	/* Select the role to be used during restore */
3401	if (ropt && ropt->use_role)
3402		ahprintf(AH, "SET ROLE %s;\n", fmtId(ropt->use_role));
3403
3404	/* Select the dump-time search_path */
3405	if (AH->public.searchpath)
3406		ahprintf(AH, "%s", AH->public.searchpath);
3407
3408	/* Make sure function checking is disabled */
3409	ahprintf(AH, "SET check_function_bodies = false;\n");
3410
3411	/* Ensure that all valid XML data will be accepted */
3412	ahprintf(AH, "SET xmloption = content;\n");
3413
3414	/* Avoid annoying notices etc */
3415	ahprintf(AH, "SET client_min_messages = warning;\n");
3416	if (!AH->public.std_strings)
3417		ahprintf(AH, "SET escape_string_warning = off;\n");
3418
3419	/* Adjust row-security state */
3420	if (ropt && ropt->enable_row_security)
3421		ahprintf(AH, "SET row_security = on;\n");
3422	else
3423		ahprintf(AH, "SET row_security = off;\n");
3424
3425	/*
3426	 * In --transaction-size mode, we should always be in a transaction when
3427	 * we begin to restore objects.
3428	 */
3429	if (ropt && ropt->txn_size > 0)
3430	{
3431		if (AH->connection)
3432			StartTransaction(&AH->public);
3433		else
3434			ahprintf(AH, "\nBEGIN;\n");
3435		AH->txnCount = 0;
3436	}
3437
3438	ahprintf(AH, "\n");
3439}
3440
3441/*
3442 * Issue a SET SESSION AUTHORIZATION command.  Caller is responsible
3443 * for updating state if appropriate.  If user is NULL or an empty string,
3444 * the specification DEFAULT will be used.
3445 */
3446static void
3447_doSetSessionAuth(ArchiveHandle *AH, const char *user)
3448{
3449	PQExpBuffer cmd = createPQExpBuffer();
3450
3451	appendPQExpBufferStr(cmd, "SET SESSION AUTHORIZATION ");
3452
3453	/*
3454	 * SQL requires a string literal here.  Might as well be correct.
3455	 */
3456	if (user && *user)
3457		appendStringLiteralAHX(cmd, user, AH);
3458	else
3459		appendPQExpBufferStr(cmd, "DEFAULT");
3460	appendPQExpBufferChar(cmd, ';');
3461
3462	if (RestoringToDB(AH))
3463	{
3464		PGresult   *res;
3465
3466		res = PQexec(AH->connection, cmd->data);
3467
3468		if (!res || PQresultStatus(res) != PGRES_COMMAND_OK)
3469			/* NOT warn_or_exit_horribly... use -O instead to skip this. */
3470			pg_fatal("could not set session user to \"%s\": %s",
3471					 user, PQerrorMessage(AH->connection));
3472
3473		PQclear(res);
3474	}
3475	else
3476		ahprintf(AH, "%s\n\n", cmd->data);
3477
3478	destroyPQExpBuffer(cmd);
3479}
3480
3481
3482/*
3483 * Issue the commands to connect to the specified database.
3484 *
3485 * If we're currently restoring right into a database, this will
3486 * actually establish a connection. Otherwise it puts a \connect into
3487 * the script output.
3488 */
3489static void
3490_reconnectToDB(ArchiveHandle *AH, const char *dbname)
3491{
3492	if (RestoringToDB(AH))
3493		ReconnectToServer(AH, dbname);
3494	else
3495	{
3496		PQExpBufferData connectbuf;
3497		RestoreOptions *ropt = AH->public.ropt;
3498
3499		/*
3500		 * We must temporarily exit restricted mode for \connect, etc.
3501		 * Anything added between this line and the following \restrict must
3502		 * be careful to avoid any possible meta-command injection vectors.
3503		 */
3504		ahprintf(AH, "\\unrestrict %s\n", ropt->restrict_key);
3505
3506		initPQExpBuffer(&connectbuf);
3507		appendPsqlMetaConnect(&connectbuf, dbname);
3508		ahprintf(AH, "%s", connectbuf.data);
3509		termPQExpBuffer(&connectbuf);
3510
3511		ahprintf(AH, "\\restrict %s\n\n", ropt->restrict_key);
3512	}
3513
3514	/*
3515	 * NOTE: currUser keeps track of what the imaginary session user in our
3516	 * script is.  It's now effectively reset to the original userID.
3517	 */
3518	free(AH->currUser);
3519	AH->currUser = NULL;
3520
3521	/* don't assume we still know the output schema, tablespace, etc either */
3522	free(AH->currSchema);
3523	AH->currSchema = NULL;
3524
3525	free(AH->currTableAm);
3526	AH->currTableAm = NULL;
3527
3528	free(AH->currTablespace);
3529	AH->currTablespace = NULL;
3530
3531	/* re-establish fixed state */
3532	_doSetFixedOutputState(AH);
3533}
3534
3535/*
3536 * Become the specified user, and update state to avoid redundant commands
3537 *
3538 * NULL or empty argument is taken to mean restoring the session default
3539 */
3540static void
3541_becomeUser(ArchiveHandle *AH, const char *user)
3542{
3543	if (!user)
3544		user = "";				/* avoid null pointers */
3545
3546	if (AH->currUser && strcmp(AH->currUser, user) == 0)
3547		return;					/* no need to do anything */
3548
3549	_doSetSessionAuth(AH, user);
3550
3551	/*
3552	 * NOTE: currUser keeps track of what the imaginary session user in our
3553	 * script is
3554	 */
3555	free(AH->currUser);
3556	AH->currUser = pg_strdup(user);
3557}
3558
3559/*
3560 * Become the owner of the given TOC entry object.  If
3561 * changes in ownership are not allowed, this doesn't do anything.
3562 */
3563static void
3564_becomeOwner(ArchiveHandle *AH, TocEntry *te)
3565{
3566	RestoreOptions *ropt = AH->public.ropt;
3567
3568	if (ropt && (ropt->noOwner || !ropt->use_setsessauth))
3569		return;
3570
3571	_becomeUser(AH, te->owner);
3572}
3573
3574
3575/*
3576 * Issue the commands to select the specified schema as the current schema
3577 * in the target database.
3578 */
3579static void
3580_selectOutputSchema(ArchiveHandle *AH, const char *schemaName)
3581{
3582	PQExpBuffer qry;
3583
3584	/*
3585	 * If there was a SEARCHPATH TOC entry, we're supposed to just stay with
3586	 * that search_path rather than switching to entry-specific paths.
3587	 * Otherwise, it's an old archive that will not restore correctly unless
3588	 * we set the search_path as it's expecting.
3589	 */
3590	if (AH->public.searchpath)
3591		return;
3592
3593	if (!schemaName || *schemaName == '\0' ||
3594		(AH->currSchema && strcmp(AH->currSchema, schemaName) == 0))
3595		return;					/* no need to do anything */
3596
3597	qry = createPQExpBuffer();
3598
3599	appendPQExpBuffer(qry, "SET search_path = %s",
3600					  fmtId(schemaName));
3601	if (strcmp(schemaName, "pg_catalog") != 0)
3602		appendPQExpBufferStr(qry, ", pg_catalog");
3603
3604	if (RestoringToDB(AH))
3605	{
3606		PGresult   *res;
3607
3608		res = PQexec(AH->connection, qry->data);
3609
3610		if (!res || PQresultStatus(res) != PGRES_COMMAND_OK)
3611			warn_or_exit_horribly(AH,
3612								  "could not set \"search_path\" to \"%s\": %s",
3613								  schemaName, PQerrorMessage(AH->connection));
3614
3615		PQclear(res);
3616	}
3617	else
3618		ahprintf(AH, "%s;\n\n", qry->data);
3619
3620	free(AH->currSchema);
3621	AH->currSchema = pg_strdup(schemaName);
3622
3623	destroyPQExpBuffer(qry);
3624}
3625
3626/*
3627 * Issue the commands to select the specified tablespace as the current one
3628 * in the target database.
3629 */
3630static void
3631_selectTablespace(ArchiveHandle *AH, const char *tablespace)
3632{
3633	RestoreOptions *ropt = AH->public.ropt;
3634	PQExpBuffer qry;
3635	const char *want,
3636			   *have;
3637
3638	/* do nothing in --no-tablespaces mode */
3639	if (ropt->noTablespace)
3640		return;
3641
3642	have = AH->currTablespace;
3643	want = tablespace;
3644
3645	/* no need to do anything for non-tablespace object */
3646	if (!want)
3647		return;
3648
3649	if (have && strcmp(want, have) == 0)
3650		return;					/* no need to do anything */
3651
3652	qry = createPQExpBuffer();
3653
3654	if (strcmp(want, "") == 0)
3655	{
3656		/* We want the tablespace to be the database's default */
3657		appendPQExpBufferStr(qry, "SET default_tablespace = ''");
3658	}
3659	else
3660	{
3661		/* We want an explicit tablespace */
3662		appendPQExpBuffer(qry, "SET default_tablespace = %s", fmtId(want));
3663	}
3664
3665	if (RestoringToDB(AH))
3666	{
3667		PGresult   *res;
3668
3669		res = PQexec(AH->connection, qry->data);
3670
3671		if (!res || PQresultStatus(res) != PGRES_COMMAND_OK)
3672			warn_or_exit_horribly(AH,
3673								  "could not set \"default_tablespace\" to %s: %s",
3674								  fmtId(want), PQerrorMessage(AH->connection));
3675
3676		PQclear(res);
3677	}
3678	else
3679		ahprintf(AH, "%s;\n\n", qry->data);
3680
3681	free(AH->currTablespace);
3682	AH->currTablespace = pg_strdup(want);
3683
3684	destroyPQExpBuffer(qry);
3685}
3686
3687/*
3688 * Set the proper default_table_access_method value for the table.
3689 */
3690static void
3691_selectTableAccessMethod(ArchiveHandle *AH, const char *tableam)
3692{
3693	RestoreOptions *ropt = AH->public.ropt;
3694	PQExpBuffer cmd;
3695	const char *want,
3696			   *have;
3697
3698	/* do nothing in --no-table-access-method mode */
3699	if (ropt->noTableAm)
3700		return;
3701
3702	have = AH->currTableAm;
3703	want = tableam;
3704
3705	if (!want)
3706		return;
3707
3708	if (have && strcmp(want, have) == 0)
3709		return;
3710
3711	cmd = createPQExpBuffer();
3712	appendPQExpBuffer(cmd, "SET default_table_access_method = %s;", fmtId(want));
3713
3714	if (RestoringToDB(AH))
3715	{
3716		PGresult   *res;
3717
3718		res = PQexec(AH->connection, cmd->data);
3719
3720		if (!res || PQresultStatus(res) != PGRES_COMMAND_OK)
3721			warn_or_exit_horribly(AH,
3722								  "could not set \"default_table_access_method\": %s",
3723								  PQerrorMessage(AH->connection));
3724
3725		PQclear(res);
3726	}
3727	else
3728		ahprintf(AH, "%s\n\n", cmd->data);
3729
3730	destroyPQExpBuffer(cmd);
3731
3732	free(AH->currTableAm);
3733	AH->currTableAm = pg_strdup(want);
3734}
3735
3736/*
3737 * Set the proper default table access method for a table without storage.
3738 * Currently, this is required only for partitioned tables with a table AM.
3739 */
3740static void
3741_printTableAccessMethodNoStorage(ArchiveHandle *AH, TocEntry *te)
3742{
3743	RestoreOptions *ropt = AH->public.ropt;
3744	const char *tableam = te->tableam;
3745	PQExpBuffer cmd;
3746
3747	/* do nothing in --no-table-access-method mode */
3748	if (ropt->noTableAm)
3749		return;
3750
3751	if (!tableam)
3752		return;
3753
3754	Assert(te->relkind == RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE);
3755
3756	cmd = createPQExpBuffer();
3757
3758	appendPQExpBufferStr(cmd, "ALTER TABLE ");
3759	appendPQExpBuffer(cmd, "%s ", fmtQualifiedId(te->namespace, te->tag));
3760	appendPQExpBuffer(cmd, "SET ACCESS METHOD %s;",
3761					  fmtId(tableam));
3762
3763	if (RestoringToDB(AH))
3764	{
3765		PGresult   *res;
3766
3767		res = PQexec(AH->connection, cmd->data);
3768
3769		if (!res || PQresultStatus(res) != PGRES_COMMAND_OK)
3770			warn_or_exit_horribly(AH,
3771								  "could not alter table access method: %s",
3772								  PQerrorMessage(AH->connection));
3773		PQclear(res);
3774	}
3775	else
3776		ahprintf(AH, "%s\n\n", cmd->data);
3777
3778	destroyPQExpBuffer(cmd);
3779}
3780
3781/*
3782 * Extract an object description for a TOC entry, and append it to buf.
3783 *
3784 * This is used for ALTER ... OWNER TO.
3785 *
3786 * If the object type has no owner, do nothing.
3787 */
3788static void
3789_getObjectDescription(PQExpBuffer buf, const TocEntry *te)
3790{
3791	const char *type = te->desc;
3792
3793	/* objects that don't require special decoration */
3794	if (strcmp(type, "COLLATION") == 0 ||
3795		strcmp(type, "CONVERSION") == 0 ||
3796		strcmp(type, "DOMAIN") == 0 ||
3797		strcmp(type, "FOREIGN TABLE") == 0 ||
3798		strcmp(type, "MATERIALIZED VIEW") == 0 ||
3799		strcmp(type, "SEQUENCE") == 0 ||
3800		strcmp(type, "STATISTICS") == 0 ||
3801		strcmp(type, "TABLE") == 0 ||
3802		strcmp(type, "TEXT SEARCH DICTIONARY") == 0 ||
3803		strcmp(type, "TEXT SEARCH CONFIGURATION") == 0 ||
3804		strcmp(type, "TYPE") == 0 ||
3805		strcmp(type, "VIEW") == 0 ||
3806	/* non-schema-specified objects */
3807		strcmp(type, "DATABASE") == 0 ||
3808		strcmp(type, "PROCEDURAL LANGUAGE") == 0 ||
3809		strcmp(type, "SCHEMA") == 0 ||
3810		strcmp(type, "EVENT TRIGGER") == 0 ||
3811		strcmp(type, "FOREIGN DATA WRAPPER") == 0 ||
3812		strcmp(type, "SERVER") == 0 ||
3813		strcmp(type, "PUBLICATION") == 0 ||
3814		strcmp(type, "SUBSCRIPTION") == 0)
3815	{
3816		appendPQExpBuffer(buf, "%s ", type);
3817		if (te->namespace && *te->namespace)
3818			appendPQExpBuffer(buf, "%s.", fmtId(te->namespace));
3819		appendPQExpBufferStr(buf, fmtId(te->tag));
3820	}
3821	/* LOs just have a name, but it's numeric so must not use fmtId */
3822	else if (strcmp(type, "BLOB") == 0)
3823	{
3824		appendPQExpBuffer(buf, "LARGE OBJECT %s", te->tag);
3825	}
3826
3827	/*
3828	 * These object types require additional decoration.  Fortunately, the
3829	 * information needed is exactly what's in the DROP command.
3830	 */
3831	else if (strcmp(type, "AGGREGATE") == 0 ||
3832			 strcmp(type, "FUNCTION") == 0 ||
3833			 strcmp(type, "OPERATOR") == 0 ||
3834			 strcmp(type, "OPERATOR CLASS") == 0 ||
3835			 strcmp(type, "OPERATOR FAMILY") == 0 ||
3836			 strcmp(type, "PROCEDURE") == 0)
3837	{
3838		/* Chop "DROP " off the front and make a modifiable copy */
3839		char	   *first = pg_strdup(te->dropStmt + 5);
3840		char	   *last;
3841
3842		/* point to last character in string */
3843		last = first + strlen(first) - 1;
3844
3845		/* Strip off any ';' or '\n' at the end */
3846		while (last >= first && (*last == '\n' || *last == ';'))
3847			last--;
3848		*(last + 1) = '\0';
3849
3850		appendPQExpBufferStr(buf, first);
3851
3852		free(first);
3853		return;
3854	}
3855	/* these object types don't have separate owners */
3856	else if (strcmp(type, "CAST") == 0 ||
3857			 strcmp(type, "CHECK CONSTRAINT") == 0 ||
3858			 strcmp(type, "CONSTRAINT") == 0 ||
3859			 strcmp(type, "DATABASE PROPERTIES") == 0 ||
3860			 strcmp(type, "DEFAULT") == 0 ||
3861			 strcmp(type, "FK CONSTRAINT") == 0 ||
3862			 strcmp(type, "INDEX") == 0 ||
3863			 strcmp(type, "RULE") == 0 ||
3864			 strcmp(type, "TRIGGER") == 0 ||
3865			 strcmp(type, "ROW SECURITY") == 0 ||
3866			 strcmp(type, "POLICY") == 0 ||
3867			 strcmp(type, "USER MAPPING") == 0)
3868	{
3869		/* do nothing */
3870	}
3871	else
3872		pg_fatal("don't know how to set owner for object type \"%s\"", type);
3873}
3874
3875/*
3876 * Emit the SQL commands to create the object represented by a TOC entry
3877 *
3878 * This now also includes issuing an ALTER OWNER command to restore the
3879 * object's ownership, if wanted.  But note that the object's permissions
3880 * will remain at default, until the matching ACL TOC entry is restored.
3881 */
3882static void
3883_printTocEntry(ArchiveHandle *AH, TocEntry *te, const char *pfx)
3884{
3885	RestoreOptions *ropt = AH->public.ropt;
3886
3887	/*
3888	 * Select owner, schema, tablespace and default AM as necessary. The
3889	 * default access method for partitioned tables is handled after
3890	 * generating the object definition, as it requires an ALTER command
3891	 * rather than SET.
3892	 */
3893	_becomeOwner(AH, te);
3894	_selectOutputSchema(AH, te->namespace);
3895	_selectTablespace(AH, te->tablespace);
3896	if (te->relkind != RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE)
3897		_selectTableAccessMethod(AH, te->tableam);
3898
3899	/* Emit header comment for item */
3900	if (!AH->noTocComments)
3901	{
3902		char	   *sanitized_name;
3903		char	   *sanitized_schema;
3904		char	   *sanitized_owner;
3905
3906		ahprintf(AH, "--\n");
3907		if (AH->public.verbose)
3908		{
3909			ahprintf(AH, "-- TOC entry %d (class %u OID %u)\n",
3910					 te->dumpId, te->catalogId.tableoid, te->catalogId.oid);
3911			if (te->nDeps > 0)
3912			{
3913				int			i;
3914
3915				ahprintf(AH, "-- Dependencies:");
3916				for (i = 0; i < te->nDeps; i++)
3917					ahprintf(AH, " %d", te->dependencies[i]);
3918				ahprintf(AH, "\n");
3919			}
3920		}
3921
3922		sanitized_name = sanitize_line(te->tag, false);
3923		sanitized_schema = sanitize_line(te->namespace, true);
3924		sanitized_owner = sanitize_line(ropt->noOwner ? NULL : te->owner, true);
3925
3926		ahprintf(AH, "-- %sName: %s; Type: %s; Schema: %s; Owner: %s",
3927				 pfx, sanitized_name, te->desc, sanitized_schema,
3928				 sanitized_owner);
3929
3930		free(sanitized_name);
3931		free(sanitized_schema);
3932		free(sanitized_owner);
3933
3934		if (te->tablespace && strlen(te->tablespace) > 0 && !ropt->noTablespace)
3935		{
3936			char	   *sanitized_tablespace;
3937
3938			sanitized_tablespace = sanitize_line(te->tablespace, false);
3939			ahprintf(AH, "; Tablespace: %s", sanitized_tablespace);
3940			free(sanitized_tablespace);
3941		}
3942		ahprintf(AH, "\n");
3943
3944		if (AH->PrintExtraTocPtr != NULL)
3945			AH->PrintExtraTocPtr(AH, te);
3946		ahprintf(AH, "--\n\n");
3947	}
3948
3949	/*
3950	 * Actually print the definition.  Normally we can just print the defn
3951	 * string if any, but we have four special cases:
3952	 *
3953	 * 1. A crude hack for suppressing AUTHORIZATION clause that old pg_dump
3954	 * versions put into CREATE SCHEMA.  Don't mutate the variant for schema
3955	 * "public" that is a comment.  We have to do this when --no-owner mode is
3956	 * selected.  This is ugly, but I see no other good way ...
3957	 *
3958	 * 2. BLOB METADATA entries need special processing since their defn
3959	 * strings are just lists of OIDs, not complete SQL commands.
3960	 *
3961	 * 3. ACL LARGE OBJECTS entries need special processing because they
3962	 * contain only one copy of the ACL GRANT/REVOKE commands, which we must
3963	 * apply to each large object listed in the associated BLOB METADATA.
3964	 *
3965	 * 4. Entries with a defnDumper need to call it to generate the
3966	 * definition.  This is primarily intended to provide a way to save memory
3967	 * for objects that would otherwise need a lot of it (e.g., statistics
3968	 * data).
3969	 */
3970	if (ropt->noOwner &&
3971		strcmp(te->desc, "SCHEMA") == 0 && strncmp(te->defn, "--", 2) != 0)
3972	{
3973		ahprintf(AH, "CREATE SCHEMA %s;\n\n\n", fmtId(te->tag));
3974	}
3975	else if (strcmp(te->desc, "BLOB METADATA") == 0)
3976	{
3977		IssueCommandPerBlob(AH, te, "SELECT pg_catalog.lo_create('", "')");
3978	}
3979	else if (strcmp(te->desc, "ACL") == 0 &&
3980			 strncmp(te->tag, "LARGE OBJECTS", 13) == 0)
3981	{
3982		IssueACLPerBlob(AH, te);
3983	}
3984	else if (te->defnLen && AH->format != archTar)
3985	{
3986		/*
3987		 * If defnLen is set, the defnDumper has already been called for this
3988		 * TOC entry.  We don't normally expect a defnDumper to be called for
3989		 * a TOC entry a second time in _printTocEntry(), but there's an
3990		 * exception.  The tar format first calls WriteToc(), which scans the
3991		 * entire TOC, and then it later calls RestoreArchive() to generate
3992		 * restore.sql, which scans the TOC again.  There doesn't appear to be
3993		 * a good way to prevent a second defnDumper call in this case without
3994		 * storing the definition in memory, which defeats the purpose.  This
3995		 * second defnDumper invocation should generate the same output as the
3996		 * first, but even if it doesn't, the worst-case scenario is that
3997		 * restore.sql might have different statistics data than the archive.
3998		 *
3999		 * In all other cases, encountering a TOC entry a second time in
4000		 * _printTocEntry() is unexpected, so we fail because one of our
4001		 * assumptions must no longer hold true.
4002		 *
4003		 * XXX This is a layering violation, but the alternative is an awkward
4004		 * and complicated callback infrastructure for this special case. This
4005		 * might be worth revisiting in the future.
4006		 */
4007		pg_fatal("unexpected TOC entry in _printTocEntry(): %d %s %s",
4008				 te->dumpId, te->desc, te->tag);
4009	}
4010	else if (te->defnDumper)
4011	{
4012		char	   *defn = te->defnDumper((Archive *) AH, te->defnDumperArg, te);
4013
4014		te->defnLen = ahprintf(AH, "%s\n\n", defn);
4015		pg_free(defn);
4016	}
4017	else if (te->defn && strlen(te->defn) > 0)
4018	{
4019		ahprintf(AH, "%s\n\n", te->defn);
4020
4021		/*
4022		 * If the defn string contains multiple SQL commands, txn_size mode
4023		 * should count it as N actions not one.  But rather than build a full
4024		 * SQL parser, approximate this by counting semicolons.  One case
4025		 * where that tends to be badly fooled is function definitions, so
4026		 * ignore them.  (restore_toc_entry will count one action anyway.)
4027		 */
4028		if (ropt->txn_size > 0 &&
4029			strcmp(te->desc, "FUNCTION") != 0 &&
4030			strcmp(te->desc, "PROCEDURE") != 0)
4031		{
4032			const char *p = te->defn;
4033			int			nsemis = 0;
4034
4035			while ((p = strchr(p, ';')) != NULL)
4036			{
4037				nsemis++;
4038				p++;
4039			}
4040			if (nsemis > 1)
4041				AH->txnCount += nsemis - 1;
4042		}
4043	}
4044
4045	/*
4046	 * If we aren't using SET SESSION AUTH to determine ownership, we must
4047	 * instead issue an ALTER OWNER command.  Schema "public" is special; when
4048	 * a dump emits a comment in lieu of creating it, we use ALTER OWNER even
4049	 * when using SET SESSION for all other objects.  We assume that anything
4050	 * without a DROP command is not a separately ownable object.
4051	 */
4052	if (!ropt->noOwner &&
4053		(!ropt->use_setsessauth ||
4054		 (strcmp(te->desc, "SCHEMA") == 0 &&
4055		  strncmp(te->defn, "--", 2) == 0)) &&
4056		te->owner && strlen(te->owner) > 0 &&
4057		te->dropStmt && strlen(te->dropStmt) > 0)
4058	{
4059		if (strcmp(te->desc, "BLOB METADATA") == 0)
4060		{
4061			/* BLOB METADATA needs special code to handle multiple LOs */
4062			char	   *cmdEnd = psprintf(" OWNER TO %s", fmtId(te->owner));
4063
4064			IssueCommandPerBlob(AH, te, "ALTER LARGE OBJECT ", cmdEnd);
4065			pg_free(cmdEnd);
4066		}
4067		else
4068		{
4069			/* For all other cases, we can use _getObjectDescription */
4070			PQExpBufferData temp;
4071
4072			initPQExpBuffer(&temp);
4073			_getObjectDescription(&temp, te);
4074
4075			/*
4076			 * If _getObjectDescription() didn't fill the buffer, then there
4077			 * is no owner.
4078			 */
4079			if (temp.data[0])
4080				ahprintf(AH, "ALTER %s OWNER TO %s;\n\n",
4081						 temp.data, fmtId(te->owner));
4082			termPQExpBuffer(&temp);
4083		}
4084	}
4085
4086	/*
4087	 * Select a partitioned table's default AM, once the table definition has
4088	 * been generated.
4089	 */
4090	if (te->relkind == RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE)
4091		_printTableAccessMethodNoStorage(AH, te);
4092
4093	/*
4094	 * If it's an ACL entry, it might contain SET SESSION AUTHORIZATION
4095	 * commands, so we can no longer assume we know the current auth setting.
4096	 */
4097	if (_tocEntryIsACL(te))
4098	{
4099		free(AH->currUser);
4100		AH->currUser = NULL;
4101	}
4102}
4103
4104/*
4105 * Write the file header for a custom-format archive
4106 */
4107void
4108WriteHead(ArchiveHandle *AH)
4109{
4110	struct tm	crtm;
4111
4112	AH->WriteBufPtr(AH, "PGDMP", 5);	/* Magic code */
4113	AH->WriteBytePtr(AH, ARCHIVE_MAJOR(AH->version));
4114	AH->WriteBytePtr(AH, ARCHIVE_MINOR(AH->version));
4115	AH->WriteBytePtr(AH, ARCHIVE_REV(AH->version));
4116	AH->WriteBytePtr(AH, AH->intSize);
4117	AH->WriteBytePtr(AH, AH->offSize);
4118	AH->WriteBytePtr(AH, AH->format);
4119	AH->WriteBytePtr(AH, AH->compression_spec.algorithm);
4120	crtm = *localtime(&AH->createDate);
4121	WriteInt(AH, crtm.tm_sec);
4122	WriteInt(AH, crtm.tm_min);
4123	WriteInt(AH, crtm.tm_hour);
4124	WriteInt(AH, crtm.tm_mday);
4125	WriteInt(AH, crtm.tm_mon);
4126	WriteInt(AH, crtm.tm_year);
4127	WriteInt(AH, crtm.tm_isdst);
4128	WriteStr(AH, PQdb(AH->connection));
4129	WriteStr(AH, AH->public.remoteVersionStr);
4130	WriteStr(AH, PG_VERSION);
4131}
4132
4133void
4134ReadHead(ArchiveHandle *AH)
4135{
4136	char	   *errmsg;
4137	char		vmaj,
4138				vmin,
4139				vrev;
4140	int			fmt;
4141
4142	/*
4143	 * If we haven't already read the header, do so.
4144	 *
4145	 * NB: this code must agree with _discoverArchiveFormat().  Maybe find a
4146	 * way to unify the cases?
4147	 */
4148	if (!AH->readHeader)
4149	{
4150		char		tmpMag[7];
4151
4152		AH->ReadBufPtr(AH, tmpMag, 5);
4153
4154		if (strncmp(tmpMag, "PGDMP", 5) != 0)
4155			pg_fatal("did not find magic string in file header");
4156	}
4157
4158	vmaj = AH->ReadBytePtr(AH);
4159	vmin = AH->ReadBytePtr(AH);
4160
4161	if (vmaj > 1 || (vmaj == 1 && vmin > 0))	/* Version > 1.0 */
4162		vrev = AH->ReadBytePtr(AH);
4163	else
4164		vrev = 0;
4165
4166	AH->version = MAKE_ARCHIVE_VERSION(vmaj, vmin, vrev);
4167
4168	if (AH->version < K_VERS_1_0 || AH->version > K_VERS_MAX)
4169		pg_fatal("unsupported version (%d.%d) in file header",
4170				 vmaj, vmin);
4171
4172	AH->intSize = AH->ReadBytePtr(AH);
4173	if (AH->intSize > 32)
4174		pg_fatal("sanity check on integer size (%lu) failed",
4175				 (unsigned long) AH->intSize);
4176
4177	if (AH->intSize > sizeof(int))
4178		pg_log_warning("archive was made on a machine with larger integers, some operations might fail");
4179
4180	if (AH->version >= K_VERS_1_7)
4181		AH->offSize = AH->ReadBytePtr(AH);
4182	else
4183		AH->offSize = AH->intSize;
4184
4185	fmt = AH->ReadBytePtr(AH);
4186
4187	if (AH->format != fmt)
4188		pg_fatal("expected format (%d) differs from format found in file (%d)",
4189				 AH->format, fmt);
4190
4191	if (AH->version >= K_VERS_1_15)
4192		AH->compression_spec.algorithm = AH->ReadBytePtr(AH);
4193	else if (AH->version >= K_VERS_1_2)
4194	{
4195		/* Guess the compression method based on the level */
4196		if (AH->version < K_VERS_1_4)
4197			AH->compression_spec.level = AH->ReadBytePtr(AH);
4198		else
4199			AH->compression_spec.level = ReadInt(AH);
4200
4201		if (AH->compression_spec.level != 0)
4202			AH->compression_spec.algorithm = PG_COMPRESSION_GZIP;
4203	}
4204	else
4205		AH->compression_spec.algorithm = PG_COMPRESSION_GZIP;
4206
4207	errmsg = supports_compression(AH->compression_spec);
4208	if (errmsg)
4209	{
4210		pg_log_warning("archive is compressed, but this installation does not support compression (%s) -- no data will be available",
4211					   errmsg);
4212		pg_free(errmsg);
4213	}
4214
4215	if (AH->version >= K_VERS_1_4)
4216	{
4217		struct tm	crtm;
4218
4219		crtm.tm_sec = ReadInt(AH);
4220		crtm.tm_min = ReadInt(AH);
4221		crtm.tm_hour = ReadInt(AH);
4222		crtm.tm_mday = ReadInt(AH);
4223		crtm.tm_mon = ReadInt(AH);
4224		crtm.tm_year = ReadInt(AH);
4225		crtm.tm_isdst = ReadInt(AH);
4226
4227		/*
4228		 * Newer versions of glibc have mktime() report failure if tm_isdst is
4229		 * inconsistent with the prevailing timezone, e.g. tm_isdst = 1 when
4230		 * TZ=UTC.  This is problematic when restoring an archive under a
4231		 * different timezone setting.  If we get a failure, try again with
4232		 * tm_isdst set to -1 ("don't know").
4233		 *
4234		 * XXX with or without this hack, we reconstruct createDate
4235		 * incorrectly when the prevailing timezone is different from
4236		 * pg_dump's.  Next time we bump the archive version, we should flush
4237		 * this representation and store a plain seconds-since-the-Epoch
4238		 * timestamp instead.
4239		 */
4240		AH->createDate = mktime(&crtm);
4241		if (AH->createDate == (time_t) -1)
4242		{
4243			crtm.tm_isdst = -1;
4244			AH->createDate = mktime(&crtm);
4245			if (AH->createDate == (time_t) -1)
4246				pg_log_warning("invalid creation date in header");
4247		}
4248	}
4249
4250	if (AH->version >= K_VERS_1_4)
4251	{
4252		AH->archdbname = ReadStr(AH);
4253	}
4254
4255	if (AH->version >= K_VERS_1_10)
4256	{
4257		AH->archiveRemoteVersion = ReadStr(AH);
4258		AH->archiveDumpVersion = ReadStr(AH);
4259	}
4260}
4261
4262
4263/*
4264 * checkSeek
4265 *	  check to see if ftell/fseek can be performed.
4266 */
4267bool
4268checkSeek(FILE *fp)
4269{
4270	pgoff_t		tpos;
4271
4272	/* Check that ftello works on this file */
4273	tpos = ftello(fp);
4274	if (tpos < 0)
4275		return false;
4276
4277	/*
4278	 * Check that fseeko(SEEK_SET) works, too.  NB: we used to try to test
4279	 * this with fseeko(fp, 0, SEEK_CUR).  But some platforms treat that as a
4280	 * successful no-op even on files that are otherwise unseekable.
4281	 */
4282	if (fseeko(fp, tpos, SEEK_SET) != 0)
4283		return false;
4284
4285	return true;
4286}
4287
4288
4289/*
4290 * dumpTimestamp
4291 */
4292static void
4293dumpTimestamp(ArchiveHandle *AH, const char *msg, time_t tim)
4294{
4295	char		buf[64];
4296
4297	if (strftime(buf, sizeof(buf), PGDUMP_STRFTIME_FMT, localtime(&tim)) != 0)
4298		ahprintf(AH, "-- %s %s\n\n", msg, buf);
4299}
4300
4301/*
4302 * Main engine for parallel restore.
4303 *
4304 * Parallel restore is done in three phases.  In this first phase,
4305 * we'll process all SECTION_PRE_DATA TOC entries that are allowed to be
4306 * processed in the RESTORE_PASS_MAIN pass.  (In practice, that's all
4307 * PRE_DATA items other than ACLs.)  Entries we can't process now are
4308 * added to the pending_list for later phases to deal with.
4309 */
4310static void
4311restore_toc_entries_prefork(ArchiveHandle *AH, TocEntry *pending_list)
4312{
4313	bool		skipped_some;
4314	TocEntry   *next_work_item;
4315
4316	pg_log_debug("entering restore_toc_entries_prefork");
4317
4318	/* Adjust dependency information */
4319	fix_dependencies(AH);
4320
4321	/*
4322	 * Do all the early stuff in a single connection in the parent. There's no
4323	 * great point in running it in parallel, in fact it will actually run
4324	 * faster in a single connection because we avoid all the connection and
4325	 * setup overhead.  Also, pre-9.2 pg_dump versions were not very good
4326	 * about showing all the dependencies of SECTION_PRE_DATA items, so we do
4327	 * not risk trying to process them out-of-order.
4328	 *
4329	 * Stuff that we can't do immediately gets added to the pending_list.
4330	 * Note: we don't yet filter out entries that aren't going to be restored.
4331	 * They might participate in dependency chains connecting entries that
4332	 * should be restored, so we treat them as live until we actually process
4333	 * them.
4334	 *
4335	 * Note: as of 9.2, it should be guaranteed that all PRE_DATA items appear
4336	 * before DATA items, and all DATA items before POST_DATA items.  That is
4337	 * not certain to be true in older archives, though, and in any case use
4338	 * of a list file would destroy that ordering (cf. SortTocFromFile).  So
4339	 * this loop cannot assume that it holds.
4340	 */
4341	AH->restorePass = RESTORE_PASS_MAIN;
4342	skipped_some = false;
4343	for (next_work_item = AH->toc->next; next_work_item != AH->toc; next_work_item = next_work_item->next)
4344	{
4345		bool		do_now = true;
4346
4347		if (next_work_item->section != SECTION_PRE_DATA)
4348		{
4349			/* DATA and POST_DATA items are just ignored for now */
4350			if (next_work_item->section == SECTION_DATA ||
4351				next_work_item->section == SECTION_POST_DATA)
4352			{
4353				do_now = false;
4354				skipped_some = true;
4355			}
4356			else
4357			{
4358				/*
4359				 * SECTION_NONE items, such as comments, can be processed now
4360				 * if we are still in the PRE_DATA part of the archive.  Once
4361				 * we've skipped any items, we have to consider whether the
4362				 * comment's dependencies are satisfied, so skip it for now.
4363				 */
4364				if (skipped_some)
4365					do_now = false;
4366			}
4367		}
4368
4369		/*
4370		 * Also skip items that need to be forced into later passes.  We need
4371		 * not set skipped_some in this case, since by assumption no main-pass
4372		 * items could depend on these.
4373		 */
4374		if (_tocEntryRestorePass(next_work_item) != RESTORE_PASS_MAIN)
4375			do_now = false;
4376
4377		if (do_now)
4378		{
4379			/* OK, restore the item and update its dependencies */
4380			pg_log_info("processing item %d %s %s",
4381						next_work_item->dumpId,
4382						next_work_item->desc, next_work_item->tag);
4383
4384			(void) restore_toc_entry(AH, next_work_item, false);
4385
4386			/* Reduce dependencies, but don't move anything to ready_heap */
4387			reduce_dependencies(AH, next_work_item, NULL);
4388		}
4389		else
4390		{
4391			/* Nope, so add it to pending_list */
4392			pending_list_append(pending_list, next_work_item);
4393		}
4394	}
4395
4396	/*
4397	 * In --transaction-size mode, we must commit the open transaction before
4398	 * dropping the database connection.  This also ensures that child workers
4399	 * can see the objects we've created so far.
4400	 */
4401	if (AH->public.ropt->txn_size > 0)
4402		CommitTransaction(&AH->public);
4403
4404	/*
4405	 * Now close parent connection in prep for parallel steps.  We do this
4406	 * mainly to ensure that we don't exceed the specified number of parallel
4407	 * connections.
4408	 */
4409	DisconnectDatabase(&AH->public);
4410
4411	/* blow away any transient state from the old connection */
4412	free(AH->currUser);
4413	AH->currUser = NULL;
4414	free(AH->currSchema);
4415	AH->currSchema = NULL;
4416	free(AH->currTablespace);
4417	AH->currTablespace = NULL;
4418	free(AH->currTableAm);
4419	AH->currTableAm = NULL;
4420}
4421
4422/*
4423 * Main engine for parallel restore.
4424 *
4425 * Parallel restore is done in three phases.  In this second phase,
4426 * we process entries by dispatching them to parallel worker children
4427 * (processes on Unix, threads on Windows), each of which connects
4428 * separately to the database.  Inter-entry dependencies are respected,
4429 * and so is the RestorePass multi-pass structure.  When we can no longer
4430 * make any entries ready to process, we exit.  Normally, there will be
4431 * nothing left to do; but if there is, the third phase will mop up.
4432 */
4433static void
4434restore_toc_entries_parallel(ArchiveHandle *AH, ParallelState *pstate,
4435							 TocEntry *pending_list)
4436{
4437	binaryheap *ready_heap;
4438	TocEntry   *next_work_item;
4439
4440	pg_log_debug("entering restore_toc_entries_parallel");
4441
4442	/* Set up ready_heap with enough room for all known TocEntrys */
4443	ready_heap = binaryheap_allocate(AH->tocCount,
4444									 TocEntrySizeCompareBinaryheap,
4445									 NULL);
4446
4447	/*
4448	 * The pending_list contains all items that we need to restore.  Move all
4449	 * items that are available to process immediately into the ready_heap.
4450	 * After this setup, the pending list is everything that needs to be done
4451	 * but is blocked by one or more dependencies, while the ready heap
4452	 * contains items that have no remaining dependencies and are OK to
4453	 * process in the current restore pass.
4454	 */
4455	AH->restorePass = RESTORE_PASS_MAIN;
4456	move_to_ready_heap(pending_list, ready_heap, AH->restorePass);
4457
4458	/*
4459	 * main parent loop
4460	 *
4461	 * Keep going until there is no worker still running AND there is no work
4462	 * left to be done.  Note invariant: at top of loop, there should always
4463	 * be at least one worker available to dispatch a job to.
4464	 */
4465	pg_log_info("entering main parallel loop");
4466
4467	for (;;)
4468	{
4469		/* Look for an item ready to be dispatched to a worker */
4470		next_work_item = pop_next_work_item(ready_heap, pstate);
4471		if (next_work_item != NULL)
4472		{
4473			/* If not to be restored, don't waste time launching a worker */
4474			if ((next_work_item->reqs & (REQ_SCHEMA | REQ_DATA | REQ_STATS)) == 0)
4475			{
4476				pg_log_info("skipping item %d %s %s",
4477							next_work_item->dumpId,
4478							next_work_item->desc, next_work_item->tag);
4479				/* Update its dependencies as though we'd completed it */
4480				reduce_dependencies(AH, next_work_item, ready_heap);
4481				/* Loop around to see if anything else can be dispatched */
4482				continue;
4483			}
4484
4485			pg_log_info("launching item %d %s %s",
4486						next_work_item->dumpId,
4487						next_work_item->desc, next_work_item->tag);
4488
4489			/* Dispatch to some worker */
4490			DispatchJobForTocEntry(AH, pstate, next_work_item, ACT_RESTORE,
4491								   mark_restore_job_done, ready_heap);
4492		}
4493		else if (IsEveryWorkerIdle(pstate))
4494		{
4495			/*
4496			 * Nothing is ready and no worker is running, so we're done with
4497			 * the current pass or maybe with the whole process.
4498			 */
4499			if (AH->restorePass == RESTORE_PASS_LAST)
4500				break;			/* No more parallel processing is possible */
4501
4502			/* Advance to next restore pass */
4503			AH->restorePass++;
4504			/* That probably allows some stuff to be made ready */
4505			move_to_ready_heap(pending_list, ready_heap, AH->restorePass);
4506			/* Loop around to see if anything's now ready */
4507			continue;
4508		}
4509		else
4510		{
4511			/*
4512			 * We have nothing ready, but at least one child is working, so
4513			 * wait for some subjob to finish.
4514			 */
4515		}
4516
4517		/*
4518		 * Before dispatching another job, check to see if anything has
4519		 * finished.  We should check every time through the loop so as to
4520		 * reduce dependencies as soon as possible.  If we were unable to
4521		 * dispatch any job this time through, wait until some worker finishes
4522		 * (and, hopefully, unblocks some pending item).  If we did dispatch
4523		 * something, continue as soon as there's at least one idle worker.
4524		 * Note that in either case, there's guaranteed to be at least one
4525		 * idle worker when we return to the top of the loop.  This ensures we
4526		 * won't block inside DispatchJobForTocEntry, which would be
4527		 * undesirable: we'd rather postpone dispatching until we see what's
4528		 * been unblocked by finished jobs.
4529		 */
4530		WaitForWorkers(AH, pstate,
4531					   next_work_item ? WFW_ONE_IDLE : WFW_GOT_STATUS);
4532	}
4533
4534	/* There should now be nothing in ready_heap. */
4535	Assert(binaryheap_empty(ready_heap));
4536
4537	binaryheap_free(ready_heap);
4538
4539	pg_log_info("finished main parallel loop");
4540}
4541
4542/*
4543 * Main engine for parallel restore.
4544 *
4545 * Parallel restore is done in three phases.  In this third phase,
4546 * we mop up any remaining TOC entries by processing them serially.
4547 * This phase normally should have nothing to do, but if we've somehow
4548 * gotten stuck due to circular dependencies or some such, this provides
4549 * at least some chance of completing the restore successfully.
4550 */
4551static void
4552restore_toc_entries_postfork(ArchiveHandle *AH, TocEntry *pending_list)
4553{
4554	RestoreOptions *ropt = AH->public.ropt;
4555	TocEntry   *te;
4556
4557	pg_log_debug("entering restore_toc_entries_postfork");
4558
4559	/*
4560	 * Now reconnect the single parent connection.
4561	 */
4562	ConnectDatabaseAhx((Archive *) AH, &ropt->cparams, true);
4563
4564	/* re-establish fixed state */
4565	_doSetFixedOutputState(AH);
4566
4567	/*
4568	 * Make sure there is no work left due to, say, circular dependencies, or
4569	 * some other pathological condition.  If so, do it in the single parent
4570	 * connection.  We don't sweat about RestorePass ordering; it's likely we
4571	 * already violated that.
4572	 */
4573	for (te = pending_list->pending_next; te != pending_list; te = te->pending_next)
4574	{
4575		pg_log_info("processing missed item %d %s %s",
4576					te->dumpId, te->desc, te->tag);
4577		(void) restore_toc_entry(AH, te, false);
4578	}
4579}
4580
4581/*
4582 * Check if te1 has an exclusive lock requirement for an item that te2 also
4583 * requires, whether or not te2's requirement is for an exclusive lock.
4584 */
4585static bool
4586has_lock_conflicts(TocEntry *te1, TocEntry *te2)
4587{
4588	int			j,
4589				k;
4590
4591	for (j = 0; j < te1->nLockDeps; j++)
4592	{
4593		for (k = 0; k < te2->nDeps; k++)
4594		{
4595			if (te1->lockDeps[j] == te2->dependencies[k])
4596				return true;
4597		}
4598	}
4599	return false;
4600}
4601
4602
4603/*
4604 * Initialize the header of the pending-items list.
4605 *
4606 * This is a circular list with a dummy TocEntry as header, just like the
4607 * main TOC list; but we use separate list links so that an entry can be in
4608 * the main TOC list as well as in the pending list.
4609 */
4610static void
4611pending_list_header_init(TocEntry *l)
4612{
4613	l->pending_prev = l->pending_next = l;
4614}
4615
4616/* Append te to the end of the pending-list headed by l */
4617static void
4618pending_list_append(TocEntry *l, TocEntry *te)
4619{
4620	te->pending_prev = l->pending_prev;
4621	l->pending_prev->pending_next = te;
4622	l->pending_prev = te;
4623	te->pending_next = l;
4624}
4625
4626/* Remove te from the pending-list */
4627static void
4628pending_list_remove(TocEntry *te)
4629{
4630	te->pending_prev->pending_next = te->pending_next;
4631	te->pending_next->pending_prev = te->pending_prev;
4632	te->pending_prev = NULL;
4633	te->pending_next = NULL;
4634}
4635
4636
4637/* qsort comparator for sorting TocEntries by dataLength */
4638static int
4639TocEntrySizeCompareQsort(const void *p1, const void *p2)
4640{
4641	const TocEntry *te1 = *(const TocEntry *const *) p1;
4642	const TocEntry *te2 = *(const TocEntry *const *) p2;
4643
4644	/* Sort by decreasing dataLength */
4645	if (te1->dataLength > te2->dataLength)
4646		return -1;
4647	if (te1->dataLength < te2->dataLength)
4648		return 1;
4649
4650	/* For equal dataLengths, sort by dumpId, just to be stable */
4651	if (te1->dumpId < te2->dumpId)
4652		return -1;
4653	if (te1->dumpId > te2->dumpId)
4654		return 1;
4655
4656	return 0;
4657}
4658
4659/* binaryheap comparator for sorting TocEntries by dataLength */
4660static int
4661TocEntrySizeCompareBinaryheap(void *p1, void *p2, void *arg)
4662{
4663	/* return opposite of qsort comparator for max-heap */
4664	return -TocEntrySizeCompareQsort(&p1, &p2);
4665}
4666
4667
4668/*
4669 * Move all immediately-ready items from pending_list to ready_heap.
4670 *
4671 * Items are considered ready if they have no remaining dependencies and
4672 * they belong in the current restore pass.  (See also reduce_dependencies,
4673 * which applies the same logic one-at-a-time.)
4674 */
4675static void
4676move_to_ready_heap(TocEntry *pending_list,
4677				   binaryheap *ready_heap,
4678				   RestorePass pass)
4679{
4680	TocEntry   *te;
4681	TocEntry   *next_te;
4682
4683	for (te = pending_list->pending_next; te != pending_list; te = next_te)
4684	{
4685		/* must save list link before possibly removing te from list */
4686		next_te = te->pending_next;
4687
4688		if (te->depCount == 0 &&
4689			_tocEntryRestorePass(te) == pass)
4690		{
4691			/* Remove it from pending_list ... */
4692			pending_list_remove(te);
4693			/* ... and add to ready_heap */
4694			binaryheap_add(ready_heap, te);
4695		}
4696	}
4697}
4698
4699/*
4700 * Find the next work item (if any) that is capable of being run now,
4701 * and remove it from the ready_heap.
4702 *
4703 * Returns the item, or NULL if nothing is runnable.
4704 *
4705 * To qualify, the item must have no remaining dependencies
4706 * and no requirements for locks that are incompatible with
4707 * items currently running.  Items in the ready_heap are known to have
4708 * no remaining dependencies, but we have to check for lock conflicts.
4709 */
4710static TocEntry *
4711pop_next_work_item(binaryheap *ready_heap,
4712				   ParallelState *pstate)
4713{
4714	/*
4715	 * Search the ready_heap until we find a suitable item.  Note that we do a
4716	 * sequential scan through the heap nodes, so even though we will first
4717	 * try to choose the highest-priority item, we might end up picking
4718	 * something with a much lower priority.  However, we expect that we will
4719	 * typically be able to pick one of the first few items, which should
4720	 * usually have a relatively high priority.
4721	 */
4722	for (int i = 0; i < binaryheap_size(ready_heap); i++)
4723	{
4724		TocEntry   *te = (TocEntry *) binaryheap_get_node(ready_heap, i);
4725		bool		conflicts = false;
4726
4727		/*
4728		 * Check to see if the item would need exclusive lock on something
4729		 * that a currently running item also needs lock on, or vice versa. If
4730		 * so, we don't want to schedule them together.
4731		 */
4732		for (int k = 0; k < pstate->numWorkers; k++)
4733		{
4734			TocEntry   *running_te = pstate->te[k];
4735
4736			if (running_te == NULL)
4737				continue;
4738			if (has_lock_conflicts(te, running_te) ||
4739				has_lock_conflicts(running_te, te))
4740			{
4741				conflicts = true;
4742				break;
4743			}
4744		}
4745
4746		if (conflicts)
4747			continue;
4748
4749		/* passed all tests, so this item can run */
4750		binaryheap_remove_node(ready_heap, i);
4751		return te;
4752	}
4753
4754	pg_log_debug("no item ready");
4755	return NULL;
4756}
4757
4758
4759/*
4760 * Restore a single TOC item in parallel with others
4761 *
4762 * this is run in the worker, i.e. in a thread (Windows) or a separate process
4763 * (everything else). A worker process executes several such work items during
4764 * a parallel backup or restore. Once we terminate here and report back that
4765 * our work is finished, the leader process will assign us a new work item.
4766 */
4767int
4768parallel_restore(ArchiveHandle *AH, TocEntry *te)
4769{
4770	int			status;
4771
4772	Assert(AH->connection != NULL);
4773
4774	/* Count only errors associated with this TOC entry */
4775	AH->public.n_errors = 0;
4776
4777	/* Restore the TOC item */
4778	status = restore_toc_entry(AH, te, true);
4779
4780	return status;
4781}
4782
4783
4784/*
4785 * Callback function that's invoked in the leader process after a step has
4786 * been parallel restored.
4787 *
4788 * Update status and reduce the dependency count of any dependent items.
4789 */
4790static void
4791mark_restore_job_done(ArchiveHandle *AH,
4792					  TocEntry *te,
4793					  int status,
4794					  void *callback_data)
4795{
4796	binaryheap *ready_heap = (binaryheap *) callback_data;
4797
4798	pg_log_info("finished item %d %s %s",
4799				te->dumpId, te->desc, te->tag);
4800
4801	if (status == WORKER_CREATE_DONE)
4802		mark_create_done(AH, te);
4803	else if (status == WORKER_INHIBIT_DATA)
4804	{
4805		inhibit_data_for_failed_table(AH, te);
4806		AH->public.n_errors++;
4807	}
4808	else if (status == WORKER_IGNORED_ERRORS)
4809		AH->public.n_errors++;
4810	else if (status != 0)
4811		pg_fatal("worker process failed: exit code %d",
4812				 status);
4813
4814	reduce_dependencies(AH, te, ready_heap);
4815}
4816
4817
4818/*
4819 * Process the dependency information into a form useful for parallel restore.
4820 *
4821 * This function takes care of fixing up some missing or badly designed
4822 * dependencies, and then prepares subsidiary data structures that will be
4823 * used in the main parallel-restore logic, including:
4824 * 1. We build the revDeps[] arrays of incoming dependency dumpIds.
4825 * 2. We set up depCount fields that are the number of as-yet-unprocessed
4826 * dependencies for each TOC entry.
4827 *
4828 * We also identify locking dependencies so that we can avoid trying to
4829 * schedule conflicting items at the same time.
4830 */
4831static void
4832fix_dependencies(ArchiveHandle *AH)
4833{
4834	TocEntry   *te;
4835	int			i;
4836
4837	/*
4838	 * Initialize the depCount/revDeps/nRevDeps fields, and make sure the TOC
4839	 * items are marked as not being in any parallel-processing list.
4840	 */
4841	for (te = AH->toc->next; te != AH->toc; te = te->next)
4842	{
4843		te->depCount = te->nDeps;
4844		te->revDeps = NULL;
4845		te->nRevDeps = 0;
4846		te->pending_prev = NULL;
4847		te->pending_next = NULL;
4848	}
4849
4850	/*
4851	 * POST_DATA items that are shown as depending on a table need to be
4852	 * re-pointed to depend on that table's data, instead.  This ensures they
4853	 * won't get scheduled until the data has been loaded.
4854	 */
4855	repoint_table_dependencies(AH);
4856
4857	/*
4858	 * Pre-8.4 versions of pg_dump neglected to set up a dependency from BLOB
4859	 * COMMENTS to BLOBS.  Cope.  (We assume there's only one BLOBS and only
4860	 * one BLOB COMMENTS in such files.)
4861	 */
4862	if (AH->version < K_VERS_1_11)
4863	{
4864		for (te = AH->toc->next; te != AH->toc; te = te->next)
4865		{
4866			if (strcmp(te->desc, "BLOB COMMENTS") == 0 && te->nDeps == 0)
4867			{
4868				TocEntry   *te2;
4869
4870				for (te2 = AH->toc->next; te2 != AH->toc; te2 = te2->next)
4871				{
4872					if (strcmp(te2->desc, "BLOBS") == 0)
4873					{
4874						te->dependencies = (DumpId *) pg_malloc(sizeof(DumpId));
4875						te->dependencies[0] = te2->dumpId;
4876						te->nDeps++;
4877						te->depCount++;
4878						break;
4879					}
4880				}
4881				break;
4882			}
4883		}
4884	}
4885
4886	/*
4887	 * At this point we start to build the revDeps reverse-dependency arrays,
4888	 * so all changes of dependencies must be complete.
4889	 */
4890
4891	/*
4892	 * Count the incoming dependencies for each item.  Also, it is possible
4893	 * that the dependencies list items that are not in the archive at all
4894	 * (that should not happen in 9.2 and later, but is highly likely in older
4895	 * archives).  Subtract such items from the depCounts.
4896	 */
4897	for (te = AH->toc->next; te != AH->toc; te = te->next)
4898	{
4899		for (i = 0; i < te->nDeps; i++)
4900		{
4901			DumpId		depid = te->dependencies[i];
4902
4903			if (depid <= AH->maxDumpId && AH->tocsByDumpId[depid] != NULL)
4904				AH->tocsByDumpId[depid]->nRevDeps++;
4905			else
4906				te->depCount--;
4907		}
4908	}
4909
4910	/*
4911	 * Allocate space for revDeps[] arrays, and reset nRevDeps so we can use
4912	 * it as a counter below.
4913	 */
4914	for (te = AH->toc->next; te != AH->toc; te = te->next)
4915	{
4916		if (te->nRevDeps > 0)
4917			te->revDeps = (DumpId *) pg_malloc(te->nRevDeps * sizeof(DumpId));
4918		te->nRevDeps = 0;
4919	}
4920
4921	/*
4922	 * Build the revDeps[] arrays of incoming-dependency dumpIds.  This had
4923	 * better agree with the loops above.
4924	 */
4925	for (te = AH->toc->next; te != AH->toc; te = te->next)
4926	{
4927		for (i = 0; i < te->nDeps; i++)
4928		{
4929			DumpId		depid = te->dependencies[i];
4930
4931			if (depid <= AH->maxDumpId && AH->tocsByDumpId[depid] != NULL)
4932			{
4933				TocEntry   *otherte = AH->tocsByDumpId[depid];
4934
4935				otherte->revDeps[otherte->nRevDeps++] = te->dumpId;
4936			}
4937		}
4938	}
4939
4940	/*
4941	 * Lastly, work out the locking dependencies.
4942	 */
4943	for (te = AH->toc->next; te != AH->toc; te = te->next)
4944	{
4945		te->lockDeps = NULL;
4946		te->nLockDeps = 0;
4947		identify_locking_dependencies(AH, te);
4948	}
4949}
4950
4951/*
4952 * Change dependencies on table items to depend on table data items instead,
4953 * but only in POST_DATA items.
4954 *
4955 * Also, for any item having such dependency(s), set its dataLength to the
4956 * largest dataLength of the table data items it depends on.  This ensures
4957 * that parallel restore will prioritize larger jobs (index builds, FK
4958 * constraint checks, etc) over smaller ones, avoiding situations where we
4959 * end a restore with only one active job working on a large table.
4960 */
4961static void
4962repoint_table_dependencies(ArchiveHandle *AH)
4963{
4964	TocEntry   *te;
4965	int			i;
4966	DumpId		olddep;
4967
4968	for (te = AH->toc->next; te != AH->toc; te = te->next)
4969	{
4970		if (te->section != SECTION_POST_DATA)
4971			continue;
4972		for (i = 0; i < te->nDeps; i++)
4973		{
4974			olddep = te->dependencies[i];
4975			if (olddep <= AH->maxDumpId &&
4976				AH->tableDataId[olddep] != 0)
4977			{
4978				DumpId		tabledataid = AH->tableDataId[olddep];
4979				TocEntry   *tabledatate = AH->tocsByDumpId[tabledataid];
4980
4981				te->dependencies[i] = tabledataid;
4982				te->dataLength = Max(te->dataLength, tabledatate->dataLength);
4983				pg_log_debug("transferring dependency %d -> %d to %d",
4984							 te->dumpId, olddep, tabledataid);
4985			}
4986		}
4987	}
4988}
4989
4990/*
4991 * Identify which objects we'll need exclusive lock on in order to restore
4992 * the given TOC entry (*other* than the one identified by the TOC entry
4993 * itself).  Record their dump IDs in the entry's lockDeps[] array.
4994 */
4995static void
4996identify_locking_dependencies(ArchiveHandle *AH, TocEntry *te)
4997{
4998	DumpId	   *lockids;
4999	int			nlockids;
5000	int			i;
5001
5002	/*
5003	 * We only care about this for POST_DATA items.  PRE_DATA items are not
5004	 * run in parallel, and DATA items are all independent by assumption.
5005	 */
5006	if (te->section != SECTION_POST_DATA)
5007		return;
5008
5009	/* Quick exit if no dependencies at all */
5010	if (te->nDeps == 0)
5011		return;
5012
5013	/*
5014	 * Most POST_DATA items are ALTER TABLEs or some moral equivalent of that,
5015	 * and hence require exclusive lock.  However, we know that CREATE INDEX
5016	 * does not.  (Maybe someday index-creating CONSTRAINTs will fall in that
5017	 * category too ... but today is not that day.)
5018	 */
5019	if (strcmp(te->desc, "INDEX") == 0)
5020		return;
5021
5022	/*
5023	 * We assume the entry requires exclusive lock on each TABLE or TABLE DATA
5024	 * item listed among its dependencies.  Originally all of these would have
5025	 * been TABLE items, but repoint_table_dependencies would have repointed
5026	 * them to the TABLE DATA items if those are present (which they might not
5027	 * be, eg in a schema-only dump).  Note that all of the entries we are
5028	 * processing here are POST_DATA; otherwise there might be a significant
5029	 * difference between a dependency on a table and a dependency on its
5030	 * data, so that closer analysis would be needed here.
5031	 */
5032	lockids = (DumpId *) pg_malloc(te->nDeps * sizeof(DumpId));
5033	nlockids = 0;
5034	for (i = 0; i < te->nDeps; i++)
5035	{
5036		DumpId		depid = te->dependencies[i];
5037
5038		if (depid <= AH->maxDumpId && AH->tocsByDumpId[depid] != NULL &&
5039			((strcmp(AH->tocsByDumpId[depid]->desc, "TABLE DATA") == 0) ||
5040			 strcmp(AH->tocsByDumpId[depid]->desc, "TABLE") == 0))
5041			lockids[nlockids++] = depid;
5042	}
5043
5044	if (nlockids == 0)
5045	{
5046		free(lockids);
5047		return;
5048	}
5049
5050	te->lockDeps = pg_realloc(lockids, nlockids * sizeof(DumpId));
5051	te->nLockDeps = nlockids;
5052}
5053
5054/*
5055 * Remove the specified TOC entry from the depCounts of items that depend on
5056 * it, thereby possibly making them ready-to-run.  Any pending item that
5057 * becomes ready should be moved to the ready_heap, if that's provided.
5058 */
5059static void
5060reduce_dependencies(ArchiveHandle *AH, TocEntry *te,
5061					binaryheap *ready_heap)
5062{
5063	int			i;
5064
5065	pg_log_debug("reducing dependencies for %d", te->dumpId);
5066
5067	for (i = 0; i < te->nRevDeps; i++)
5068	{
5069		TocEntry   *otherte = AH->tocsByDumpId[te->revDeps[i]];
5070
5071		Assert(otherte->depCount > 0);
5072		otherte->depCount--;
5073
5074		/*
5075		 * It's ready if it has no remaining dependencies, and it belongs in
5076		 * the current restore pass, and it is currently a member of the
5077		 * pending list (that check is needed to prevent double restore in
5078		 * some cases where a list-file forces out-of-order restoring).
5079		 * However, if ready_heap == NULL then caller doesn't want any list
5080		 * memberships changed.
5081		 */
5082		if (otherte->depCount == 0 &&
5083			_tocEntryRestorePass(otherte) == AH->restorePass &&
5084			otherte->pending_prev != NULL &&
5085			ready_heap != NULL)
5086		{
5087			/* Remove it from pending list ... */
5088			pending_list_remove(otherte);
5089			/* ... and add to ready_heap */
5090			binaryheap_add(ready_heap, otherte);
5091		}
5092	}
5093}
5094
5095/*
5096 * Set the created flag on the DATA member corresponding to the given
5097 * TABLE member
5098 */
5099static void
5100mark_create_done(ArchiveHandle *AH, TocEntry *te)
5101{
5102	if (AH->tableDataId[te->dumpId] != 0)
5103	{
5104		TocEntry   *ted = AH->tocsByDumpId[AH->tableDataId[te->dumpId]];
5105
5106		ted->created = true;
5107	}
5108}
5109
5110/*
5111 * Mark the DATA member corresponding to the given TABLE member
5112 * as not wanted
5113 */
5114static void
5115inhibit_data_for_failed_table(ArchiveHandle *AH, TocEntry *te)
5116{
5117	pg_log_info("table \"%s\" could not be created, will not restore its data",
5118				te->tag);
5119
5120	if (AH->tableDataId[te->dumpId] != 0)
5121	{
5122		TocEntry   *ted = AH->tocsByDumpId[AH->tableDataId[te->dumpId]];
5123
5124		ted->reqs = 0;
5125	}
5126}
5127
5128/*
5129 * Clone and de-clone routines used in parallel restoration.
5130 *
5131 * Enough of the structure is cloned to ensure that there is no
5132 * conflict between different threads each with their own clone.
5133 */
5134ArchiveHandle *
5135CloneArchive(ArchiveHandle *AH)
5136{
5137	ArchiveHandle *clone;
5138
5139	/* Make a "flat" copy */
5140	clone = (ArchiveHandle *) pg_malloc(sizeof(ArchiveHandle));
5141	memcpy(clone, AH, sizeof(ArchiveHandle));
5142
5143	/* Likewise flat-copy the RestoreOptions, so we can alter them locally */
5144	clone->public.ropt = (RestoreOptions *) pg_malloc(sizeof(RestoreOptions));
5145	memcpy(clone->public.ropt, AH->public.ropt, sizeof(RestoreOptions));
5146
5147	/* Handle format-independent fields */
5148	memset(&(clone->sqlparse), 0, sizeof(clone->sqlparse));
5149
5150	/* The clone will have its own connection, so disregard connection state */
5151	clone->connection = NULL;
5152	clone->connCancel = NULL;
5153	clone->currUser = NULL;
5154	clone->currSchema = NULL;
5155	clone->currTableAm = NULL;
5156	clone->currTablespace = NULL;
5157
5158	/* savedPassword must be local in case we change it while connecting */
5159	if (clone->savedPassword)
5160		clone->savedPassword = pg_strdup(clone->savedPassword);
5161
5162	/* clone has its own error count, too */
5163	clone->public.n_errors = 0;
5164
5165	/* clones should not share lo_buf */
5166	clone->lo_buf = NULL;
5167
5168	/*
5169	 * Clone connections disregard --transaction-size; they must commit after
5170	 * each command so that the results are immediately visible to other
5171	 * workers.
5172	 */
5173	clone->public.ropt->txn_size = 0;
5174
5175	/*
5176	 * Connect our new clone object to the database, using the same connection
5177	 * parameters used for the original connection.
5178	 */
5179	ConnectDatabaseAhx((Archive *) clone, &clone->public.ropt->cparams, true);
5180
5181	/* re-establish fixed state */
5182	if (AH->mode == archModeRead)
5183		_doSetFixedOutputState(clone);
5184	/* in write case, setupDumpWorker will fix up connection state */
5185
5186	/* Let the format-specific code have a chance too */
5187	clone->ClonePtr(clone);
5188
5189	Assert(clone->connection != NULL);
5190	return clone;
5191}
5192
5193/*
5194 * Release clone-local storage.
5195 *
5196 * Note: we assume any clone-local connection was already closed.
5197 */
5198void
5199DeCloneArchive(ArchiveHandle *AH)
5200{
5201	/* Should not have an open database connection */
5202	Assert(AH->connection == NULL);
5203
5204	/* Clear format-specific state */
5205	AH->DeClonePtr(AH);
5206
5207	/* Clear state allocated by CloneArchive */
5208	if (AH->sqlparse.curCmd)
5209		destroyPQExpBuffer(AH->sqlparse.curCmd);
5210
5211	/* Clear any connection-local state */
5212	free(AH->currUser);
5213	free(AH->currSchema);
5214	free(AH->currTablespace);
5215	free(AH->currTableAm);
5216	free(AH->savedPassword);
5217
5218	free(AH);
5219}
